runners.spotlight: Six-time winner is 4-22 on turf; last two wins have come at the Curragh but has run well in defeat over this C&D on more than one occasion; shaped well at Bellewstown on penultimate but needs to bounce back from poor Curragh run latest; 2lb above highest winning mark.
runners.comment: 4-22 on turf, has run well in defeat over C&D, remains 2lb above highest winning mark

runners.name: Howd'yadoit
runners.spotlight: 3yo won twice last year, a 5f Down Royal maiden (good) and the Ballyhane Stakes at Naas (6f, gd-yld); pleasing run on seasonal return and handicap debut at Naas last month (6f, good), beaten 3l into fourth of 13 after racing keenly; 1lb lower now and drops in trip; leading chance.
runners.comment: Won Ballyhane Stakes last year, pleasing run on return/handicap debut, leading chance

runners.name: Lethimfly
runners.spotlight: No joy since landing a 5f Bellewstown 2yo maiden (good) in 2024; only seen twice last season; fine run on reappearance at Dundalk (5f) in March when a 0.75l second (Cuban Grey 4.75l behind); same mark here and can go well if backing that up on return to turf.
runners.comment: Had Cuban Grey behind when running well on return at Dundalk, same mark here

runners.name: Midnight Dusk
runners.spotlight: Won 2yo AW maiden (6f) on debut for this yard in December; stayed on well for a 2l fifth of 14 on handicap debut at Naas latest (6f, good) in first-time cheekpieces (retained); eased 1lb and will be doing best work at the finish dropped back to 5f.
runners.comment: Stayed on for fifth at Naas latest (6f), eased 1lb, will be doing best work at finish

runners.name: Alfred Tennyson
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; 7f Limerick maiden winner for Aidan O'Brien; best run for this yard on latest when beaten 0.75l into third of 10 at Dundalk (6f) in March off 75; 5lb higher mark now returned to turf but rider claims 10lb; could go well if gets a strong pace to aim at back at 5f.
runners.comment: Best run for current yard on latest at Dundalk (6f), 5lb higher on turf but could go well

runners.name: Nouvel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins include one over C&D (yielding) off 5lb lower; sole win last year at Cork (5f, gd-yld) and was beaten just 0.5l off the same mark at Naas on latest (5f, good); raised 3lb for that and this is a deeper race.
runners.comment: C&D winner, raised 3lb for close third at Naas on latest, this is a stronger race
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: Mary Shoelaces
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins last year, off 63 at Newmarket (good) in August and off 67 on her final start at the Curragh (yielding) for this rider; career high mark now and record fresh suggests she'll need this run after a 212-day absence.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins last year, returns off career high mark and may need this after 212 days off

runners.name: Nezeeh
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard; kept busy last year and four wins included one over C&D (good) in September off 77 and just 1lb higher here; subsequently won off revised mark of 84 at Bellewstown in October; badly out of form so far this year, however, and needs a revival.
runners.comment: Four wins last year included one over C&D off 1lb higher, badly out of form this season

runners.name: Sommelier
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; dangerously well-handicapped on best form for Marco Botti; signs of revival last two starts; ran better than the bare result on latest when drawn on the wrong side at Naas; eased another 3lb and the drop back to 5f is a plus; go well.
runners.comment: Ran better than bare result on latest at Naas, eased 3lb, drop back to 5f a plus, go well

runners.name: Harry's Hill
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner on turf including once over C&D; sole success last season in Tipperary rated race (5f, good) when he had Eclairage 1.25l behind in third and weighted to uphold that form; runner up in this last year on seasonal return off 3lb higher; can't discount.
runners.comment: Eight-time winner inc' over C&D, second in this last year off 3lb higher, can't discount

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Yeats Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: The most experienced of these, having won Curragh 2yo maiden and twice campaigned in Group company, notably fifth in Group 1 Criterium International; underwhelming seasonal debut, however, in Epsom Classic Trial last month, weakening quite tamely final furlong; blinkers now tried and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Highest-rated but bit to prove after below-par seasonal return at Epsom
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Limestone
runners.spotlight: Progressed nicely in each of three juvenile runs, notably making all in Listowel rated race on heavy ground (1m); followed up on seasonal return when tongue-tied in 1m2f Cork conditions last month, dictating steady pace then battling well; Derby entry looks the type to keep progressing and could well improve for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Race to suit when winning at Cork; type to progress and could improve for this longer trip

runners.name: Asakir
runners.spotlight: Zarak gelding backed up a promising Curragh debut in March when made plenty of use of in beating a subsequent winner in 1m2f Leopardstown maiden last month, both those runs on softish ground; big step up in trip here and faces quicker conditions now.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden win franked; big step up in trip here and faces quicker ground
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ulysses colt didn't run at two but three runs already this term, showing plenty of improvement to land Ballinrobe maiden over this trip eight days ago, making virtually all to win handsomely (good-to-yielding); yard took same route with last year's winner Carmers en route to an Ascot success so demands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Impressive Ballinrobe maiden winner; Carmers completed same double for yard last year

runners.name: Sangreal
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly a 270,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Roi De France (Listed-placed 1m inc AW; RPR 112) and Vaguely Royal (1m4f Flat inc AW, 100; 1m7f hurdle); dam 1m/8.5f winner (inc AW 2yo/US turf stakes; 97); promising debut run in Leopardstown fillies maiden last month when chasing home a clear winner over 1m2f; should improve and win races but this looks a big jump in grade.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden second should improve and win races but this looks a big jump in grade

runners.name: Star Of Tara
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in four maidens at two; completely out of her depth.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in four maidens at two; completely out of her depth

races.race_name: Royal County Handicap

runners.name: Madbadanddangerous
runners.spotlight: Close second in Tipperary maiden (7.5f, yielding) on debut; off the mark on next start in 7f Naas maiden (soft); 2l third in valuable auction series final over same C&D (soft); narrowly denied on seasonal return/handicap debut at Leopardstown latest (10.5f, gd-yld); reliable and in the mix again off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Solid form as a 2yo, fine run on h'cap debut on return, in the mix again off 3lb higher

runners.name: Johnny Soda
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at Cork (1m, soft) on latest, outpaced before coming from the back to score by 0.5l; looked better the further he went so this longer trip should suit; this is harder but should go well again off 5lb higher if handles quicker ground (brother won on good to firm).
runners.comment: Stayed on strongly to win at Cork (1m), up 5lb, go well again if handles quicker ground

runners.name: Manton Bay
runners.spotlight: Built on promising debut run at the Curragh (6f, yld-sft) to win a Dundalk maiden (1m) last November; pleasing run on handicap debut/seasonal return at Naas last month (1m, good) when beaten 0.5l; raised 2lb; should stay this far on breeding; chance.
runners.comment: 1m Dundalk maiden winner, fine run on 1m h'cap debut latest, should stay this far, chance

runners.name: Now Maybe
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; struggled on heavy on debut; beaten a nose over 7f at Dundalk in November; 3l third of 18 in C&D maiden on seasonal return, close up behind 83 rated rival; opening mark doesn't look a gift but very much respected.
runners.comment: Close up behind 83 rated rival here latest, opening mark doesn't look a gift but respected

runners.name: Santa Bravado
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; best effort a 2.75l fifth of 16 on debut at the Curragh (1m, yielding); held by Manton Bay on Dundalk running in November; mildly promising run on return over 1m2f at Naas; potential improver worth a market check.
runners.comment: Handicap debutant, promise in maidens at 8f-1m2f, worth a market check

runners.name: Fallacy
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; didn't show much in a trio of 1m maidens on the AW last year and on good to yielding at Cork on return; probably best watched upped in trip unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Modest form in 1m AW and turf maidens, best watched up in trip unless market speaks

runners.name: Minella Rockett
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; promise in Tipperary maiden last August; proved that was no fluke with a 1l third behind Manton Bay at Dundalk (1m) in November; denied by a short head over the same C&D later that month; should stay this far on breeding and the form of his last run has worked out; chance if ready after break.
runners.comment: Two excellent runs over 1m at Dundalk in Nov', form of latest run solid, chance if fit

races.race_name: Book Your Summer BBQ On June 7th Maiden

runners.name: Genchev
runners.spotlight: Encouraging third behind Daytona on debut in 1m Listed race at Naas (good) last July; finished the season rated 83 after 3l fourth of 12 at Tipperary (1m1f, gd-yld); kept on nicely for a 2.25l second behind race-fit odds on rival over C&D (gd-yld) last month; now rated 87 and the one to beat.
runners.comment: Rated 87, 2.25l second behind race-fit odds on rival over C&D on return, the one to beat
runners.owner: David Spratt & Sean Jones & Mrs Lynne Lyons

runners.name: Reynir
runners.spotlight: Okay effort on debut at Gowran (9.5f, soft) but more like it on quicker ground upped to this trip at Cork when a 2.25l third; that form was franked by the fifth Trojan Fighter at Ballinrobe last week; can give Genchev most to think about.
runners.comment: Improved for better ground at Cork latest, form has been boosted, the main danger
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Louisiana
runners.spotlight: King of Change filly; unsold for E12,000 as a yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Morlock (7f AW 2yo; RPR 83) and Ramadas (Italian 1m/9.5f inc 2yo); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Canadian Grade 1 winner; likely to improve for this initial experience; watch.
runners.comment: King Of Change filly, dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Canadian Gr' 1 winner, watch

runners.name: Madam Justice
runners.spotlight: Inns Of Court filly unseated her rider and bolted before her intended debut at Leopardstown and was withdrawn; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Unseated her rider and bolted before her intended debut, can only be watched for now

runners.name: Mr Ballynonty
runners.spotlight: Ten Sovereigns gelding; E34,000 foal; half-brother to US winners Six Cider (1m turf) and Lime Beach (8.5f dirt); dam 7f 2yo Group 3 winner (stayed 1m4f; RPR 113); worth market check for in form stable.
runners.comment: E34,000 Ten Sovereigns gelding, dam 7f Group 3 winner, yard in form, market can guide

runners.name: Tanzanite Diamond
runners.spotlight: Home-bred Galileo Gold gelding; half-brother to six winners inc Roca Tumu (7f/1m inc 2yo/HK Group 2; RPR 117), Roca Rojo (7f-8.5f inc US Grade 2; 111), Roca Roma (1m; 105) and Merisi Diamond (6f-7.5f; 89); dam well beaten 1m6f Flat; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Galileo Gold gelding, half-brother to two Gr' 2 winners, worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Strike De Vega
runners.spotlight: Modest effort on debut at Cork when well held behind Almazann; hard to see him turning the tables.
runners.comment: Modest effort on debut when well behind Almazann, hard to see him turn that around

runners.name: Nosmokewithoutfire
runners.spotlight: 80-1 and tailed of on debut at Naas (7f, yld-sft); hard to fancy on that evidence.
runners.comment: 80-1 and failed to beat a rival on debut at Naas, hard to fancy
runners.owner: Ms Denise McCourt & Stephen Sweeney & Maire Bailey

races.race_name: Wexford Racecourse Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Dromana Mist
runners.spotlight: Unseated at the first on debut at Tramore but the RPR she posted at Cork (2m2f, good) when seventh of 14 last week puts her firmly in the mix with her age allowance.
runners.comment: RPR she posted on her second start last time puts her firmly in the mix

runners.name: Lady Fromentro
runners.spotlight: Made little impression here on debut (2m, soft) and tailed off at Fairyhouse last time; in good hands but others preferred.
runners.comment: Made little impression here on debut and tailed off last time; others preferred

runners.name: Shing Shang Lily
runners.spotlight: Consistent form in defeat, including when fifth of 12 at Clonmel last time (2m4f, good); tongue tie goes on and she's entitled to play a part here.
runners.comment: Consistent form in defeat gives her claims here
runners.owner: Paul A Finnegan & Michael C Finnegan & Pat O'Sullivan

runners.name: Dictionary Corner
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete two starts for Pat Flynn; tailed off at Cork on stable debut, but there was more promise in her 22l sixth back there (2m3f, good) latest; chance.
runners.comment: Best run came last time when a 22l sixth at Cork; chance

runners.name: Ersudaly
runners.spotlight: Winner of a point-to-point at the fifth attempt; 22l sixth of eight on rules' debut at Tramore was the best of her four runs over timber; fell at the fifth when in rear on handicap debut at here last time; hood worn that day now left off; could get involved.
runners.comment: Point winner at the fifth attempt fell here on h'cap bow last time; could get involved

runners.name: Moonlight Paradise
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner ran her best race under rules on debut at Clonmel (2m4f, yielding) when a 15.5l seventh; below that in two subsequent runs; top-rated and big player for local yard.
runners.comment: Point winner is top-rated and a big player for local yard

runners.name: Hunskelper
runners.spotlight: Some promise in her debut sixth in a Thurles bumper in March last year, but two runs last season were poor; makes stable/hurdling debut now.
runners.comment: Some promise in debut sixth but two runs last season poor; makes stable/hurdles debut

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in last two starts and a lot to find based on her mark; declared reserve in the 6.20 at Clonmel on Thursday.
runners.comment: Pulled up in last two starts and a lot to find based on her mark

runners.name: Strand Street Lass
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete either point-to-point and hasn't shown much in her runs under rules' either; tongue tie goes on but others preferred.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in points or starts under rules'; others preferred

runners.name: Rubi Tuesday
runners.spotlight: Beaten 18l on handicap debut at Cork and her mark of 89 means she has a bit to find.
runners.comment: Beaten 18l on handicap debut at Cork and her mark of 89 means she has a bit to find

runners.name: Scathach
runners.spotlight: Finished in midfield in a strong Leopardstown maiden on debut; remote sixth behind Bambino Fever at Fairyhouse; made mistakes and fell at two out at Naas last time; not discounted.
runners.comment: Finished in midfield at Leopardstown and Fairyhouse but fell last time; not discounted

runners.name: Keep The Head
runners.spotlight: Hard to fancy on her form to date, even at this modest level; pulled up at Clonmel when last seen in December.
runners.comment: Hard to fancy on form, even at this modest level; PU when last seen in Dec'

races.race_name: Forth Mountain Trails Mares Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Simple Things
runners.spotlight: Made a good impression when third at Fairyhouse first time (2m, soft to heavy); failed to repeat that until taking the second spot at Bellewstown last month (2m1f, yielding); not the most fluent jumper but in the mix with improvement.
runners.comment: Second at Bellewstown last month in second h'cap; in the mix with improvement

runners.name: Miss Lia
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of a Tramore maiden hurdle in 2024 (2m5f, good to yielding); some good runs in defeat last season, with multiple runner-up finishes up to 2m7f on different ground types (including over C&D); bounced back from a poor run at Fairyhouse when filling the second spot once again at Downpatrick (2m6f, yielding to soft) in March.
runners.comment: Good runs in defeat last season and again filled the second spot last time; chance
runners.owner: Sussex By The Sea Syndicate/David L'Estrange

runners.name: Kimy
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-24 is off-putting; returned to scene of her win when breaking a poor run of form at Tramore (2m1f, good to yielding), finishing third of 16; ran on well that day, but she needs to repeat it.
runners.comment: Record of 1-24 is off-putting; broke poor run of form when second last time; must repeat

runners.name: Morell River
runners.spotlight: Off the mark off 6lb lower over C&D in October (2m4f, yielding); struggled on heavy ground at Gowran (2m4f) the following month; improved with racing last season, so may need this but not dismissed.
runners.comment: Off the mark over C&D in Oct' off 6lb lower; may need this but not dismissed

runners.name: Born Bright
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner was in good form over fences last summer, winning twice; has won after a break before, so she's in the mix on return.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner added two chase victories over fences last summer; in the mix

runners.name: Lily Milan
runners.spotlight: First reserve; point-to-point winner showed signs of ability in bumpers but her maiden hurdle runs were below that.
runners.comment: Reserve; point winner showed signs of ability in bumpers but m'den hurdle runs below that

runners.name: Two Pair
runners.spotlight: Built on promising bumper and hurdle runs to score at Bellewstown last time after four months off (2m1f, yielding); kept on well that day; player on handicap debut for local trainer.
runners.comment: Kept on well to win last time after four months off; player on h'cap bow for local yard

runners.name: Fair Damsel
runners.spotlight: A tailed off finish in a Tramore handicap last time was not the greatest stable debut; best watched for the moment.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a Tramore h'cap on stable debut; best watched for the moment

runners.name: Crystal Gold
runners.spotlight: Definite ability in both completed maiden hurdles at Punchestown (2m4f, soft to heavy) when third of 24 and when fourth of 17 at Clonmel (2m1f, heavy); below her best at Cork back from a break and tongue tie goes on now.
runners.comment: Definite ability in m'dens, although below best last time; tongue tie on for h'cap bow

runners.name: Lakewood Rose
runners.spotlight: Well held in a Tramore handicap off 1lb higher last time; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Well held in a Tramore handicap off 1lb higher last time; improvement needed

runners.name: Last Note
runners.spotlight: Best hurdle run for Nick Littmoden came in a maiden contest at Market Rasen when a clear second of eight (2m5f, good); took a heavy defeat on handicap debut at Worcester (2m4f, good); more needed on stable debut.
runners.comment: Second in a m'den was her best run over hurdles; didn't repeat that in two runs since

runners.name: Joshua's Daylami
runners.spotlight: Won a weak 2m7f heavy ground Punchestown maiden in February last year; struggled on handicap debut at Cork when last seen; best watched on return from over a year off.
runners.comment: Struggled on h'cap bow when last seen over a year ago; best watched

runners.name: One Night Standard
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner for Gavin Cromwell; had been running some okay races over fences in her final runs for her former yard; back down to last winning mark now back over timber.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner for Gavin Cromwell is back down to last winning mark

runners.name: Malikanna
runners.spotlight: Left moderate bumper and maiden form behind when second at Limerick (2m, soft), outpaced before keeping on well; on that evidence, this step up in trip can help; 5lb rise is perhaps harsh, but she's a player.
runners.comment: Left moderate form behind when runner-up on h'cap debut; step up in trip can help
runners.owner: Eamon Nolan & Mrs Catherine Anne Nolan

runners.name: Game And Glory
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual hurdles winner reverted to timber at Thurles last time when 66-1 and pulled up
runners.comment: Reserve; dual hurdles winner reverted to timber at Thurles last time when 66-1 and PU

runners.name: Roxhill Roise
runners.spotlight: Totally lost form since adding second hurdles win at Tramore in November 2024; tongue tie on now, but others more likely.
runners.comment: Totally lost form since adding second hurdles win in 2024; others more likely

races.race_name: Slaney River Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Pleasureofpressure
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; maiden has been consistent in handicaps; runner-up at Tramore off this mark in November (2m5f, soft); below that when a beaten favourite at Down Royal next time (2m4f, yielding), finishing a distant third; another good effort when fourth back there last time; maybe the cheekpieces can get a bit more from him.
runners.comment: M'den has been consistent in h'caps; maybe cheekpieces can get a bit more from him

runners.name: Miss Donna
runners.spotlight: Last of eight in a maiden here in April (2m4f, good to yielding) and pulled up on handicap debut at Kilbeggan last time; others more likely.
runners.comment: Last of eight in a m'den here before PU on h'cap bow latest; others more likely

runners.name: Demanding Gerry
runners.spotlight: First reserve; runner-up in handicaps at Downpatrick (2m2f) and Kilbeggan (2m3f) in quick succession on better ground in July; did it again at Clonmel in October (2m4f, yielding); unseen since finishing slightly lame at Punchestown; chance if she returns in the same form.
runners.comment: Reserve; runner-up in three consecutive races last season; chance if in same form

runners.name: Deed Pole
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts for his handicap hurdling debut at Punchestown in October when last seen over timber but has previously finished runner-up in a Tramore maiden hurdle (2m1f, good); Flat return at Limerick when third was encouraging for his chances here.
runners.comment: Third on the Flat for his return at Limerick was encouraging for his chances here

runners.name: Mo Ghille Mar
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; three-time Flat winner for Jessica Harrington on good/good to firm; moderate hurdles form for new yard, but her sixth of ten at Bellewstown last time was not her worst effort; in this lower grade for first time.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner has run moderately in four starts for new yard

runners.name: The Dancen Kid
runners.spotlight: Went closest in a higher grade at Leopardstown in March (2m3f, soft to heavy); didn't repeat that next time here (2m, soft) and pulled up at Kilbeggan last month; needs to give his best.
runners.comment: Went closest in a higher grade at Leopardstown but couldn't repeat that in two runs since

runners.name: Dontdooddson
runners.spotlight: Veteran added a fourth win when landing a C&D handicap in July (good) off this mark; third of 16 at Kilbeggan last time suggests he still retains some level of ability.
runners.comment: C&D win last July was fourth victory over hurdles; veteran but retains some ability
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Liberated Light
runners.spotlight: Flat winner was successful over hurdles at Limerick in December 2024 off 7lb higher; moderate recent form until a change of tactics almost saw him make all at Tramore (2m1f, good to yielding) when headed late home in this headgear combination; could go one better if adopting the same tactics.
runners.comment: Moderate recent form until almost made all in a change of tactics at Tramore last time

runners.name: Doyen Og
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; good third at Bellewstown in August (2m1f, good) but didn't build on that in subsequent runs.
runners.comment: Reserve; good third in Aug' but didn't build on that in subsequent runs

runners.name: Any Chance
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; left poor maiden form behind when a 5l fifth at Limerick last time (2m, soft) on his third start; that's something to build on now in handicaps.
runners.comment: Reserve; left poor m'den form behind when fifth last time; something to build on

runners.name: Garrick Painter
runners.spotlight: Returned from 181-day absence at Perth (2m, good) in June last year to get off the mark over hurdles with an easy victory off 95 when a well-backed 15-8 favourite; posted a fine third off top weight on the Flat at the Galway festival when last seen; big player but market will be crucial after his absence.
runners.comment: Returned from absence to win at Perth in June; big player but market crucial after absence

runners.name: Boogie Inthejungle
runners.spotlight: Regressive Flat maiden didn't show much in her opening hurdle runs but a 12l sixth at Limerick last time, when 125-1 was more promising; handicap debutante.
runners.comment: Didn't show much in opening runs but a 12l sixth last time was slightly more promising

runners.name: Bohemian Bay
runners.spotlight: Didn't jump with fluency for handicap debut at Clonmel (2m1f, heavy) but kept on for a moderate fifth; recent Limerick run was a step backwards.
runners.comment: Kept on for moderate fifth on h'cap bow but Limerick run was a step backwards

runners.name: Young And Foolish
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; tailed off in all three maiden hurdle starts at 250-1; pulled up in handicap debut at Naas at 80-1 (3m, soft to heavy); cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Tailed off in all three m'dens and PU on h'cap bow at Naas

runners.name: Deise Crystal
runners.spotlight: Went well for a long way on handicap debut at Naas when fourth to a well-treated winner; disappointed at Cork next time; claims if returning to his best.
runners.comment: Went well for a long way on h'cap bow but PU at Cork next time; claims at his best

runners.name: Ray Gun
runners.spotlight: Won at a modest level on AW in Britain; weak maiden hurdle form; always behind for his first handicap hurdle at Clonmel when pulled up.
runners.comment: Modest British AW winner; weak m'den hurdle form and PU in a handicap last time

runners.name: Ballindenisk
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; beat Grade 2 winner Kazansky in a Lisronagh point-to-point last year; however, form under rules to date has been underwhelming; may show something now in a handicap.
runners.comment: Beat a subsequent Gr. 2 winner in a point but poor under rules' so far; handicap debutant
runners.owner: Mrs D W Davenport & Miss F L Garland

races.race_name: Slaney River Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Bossing It
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last summer over 2m and was third in handicaps at Tramore (2m5f, good) and Galway (2m1f, good to yielding); not at same level in more recent runs, tailed off at Tramore last time (2m1f, good to yielding).
runners.comment: In good form last summer but poor in more recent runs

runners.name: Fedneys Park
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner for Mick Goff; last month's Bellewstown (2m1f, yielding) fourth was her best race yet and provides hope that she may deliver on early promise now in a lower grade.
runners.comment: Point winner ran her best race when fourth last time; enters lower grade for first time

runners.name: Menas Miracle
runners.spotlight: Debut point winner got off the mark over hurdles when successful over C&D (good) in July off 78; unable to go in again off revised mark in two subsequent starts here and at Downpatrick; creditable efforts over fences when last seen in October.
runners.comment: C&D winner last July was unable to score again off revised mark next two times

runners.name: Gamigin
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner returned to form when runner-up at Down Royal (2m1, soft, 7-2 fav') in final start for Gary McGill in January; unfancied and well beaten in two runs for new yard; may go well back in 0-100 company now.
runners.comment: Unfancied and well beaten in two starts for new yard but back in 0-100 company now

runners.name: Cholo
runners.spotlight: Is 0-5 over hurdles but respectable runs in 2m1f Tramore contests the last twice since switching to handicaps; place chance; declared reserve for 5.50 at Clonmel on Thursday.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but respectable Tramore runs last twice since switching to h'caps

runners.name: Butch Coolidge
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; nice pedigree and has shown small hints of ability in maidens; hood goes on for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Reserve; nicely bred and small hints of ability of m'dens; hood goes on for h'cap bow

runners.name: Lost Features
runners.spotlight: Huge odds for all five runs under rules' (four in m'den hurdles) and ran poorly; hard to fancy on the basis of those now sent handicapping.
runners.comment: Huge odds for all five starts and ran like it; hard to fancy on the basis of those
runners.owner: Profit In Your Pocket Racing Club

runners.name: Final Decision
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan bumper in 2023; returned from 558 days off at Ballinrobe last April when tailed off in a maiden hurdle; another long absence to overcome on handicap/stable debut.
runners.comment: 2023 bumper winner has had long absences since and last seen tailed off last year

runners.name: Annie's Belle
runners.spotlight: Although weak in the market for her handicap debut here (2m4f, good to yielding, 40-1) last month, she was still in contention when falling at two out; chance if building on that
runners.comment: Still in contention when falling at two out here on h'cap bow; chance if building on it

runners.name: Avandra
runners.spotlight: Moderate sort now 0-16 hurdling, continues to struggle off this basement mark; blinkers are employed now.
runners.comment: Moderate sort continues to struggle off this basement mark

runners.name: Western Opera
runners.spotlight: First reserve; poor in handicaps overall but took the runner-up spot at Ballinrobe (2m6f, good) on his penultimate start last summer; this might be on the short side for stable debut.
runners.comment: Reserve; poor in h'caps except for runner-up finish in penultimate start last summer

runners.name: Comanche Moon
runners.spotlight: Now 0-9 hurdling, last five defeats in handicaps; down 5lb from initial opening mark but needs to improve a good bit to play a major role.
runners.comment: 0-9 hurdling, last five defeats in h'caps; improvement needed to play a major role

runners.name: Craic Eile
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins came at Tramore but nothing since 2021; out of form in three runs last summer.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins came at Tramore, but no wins since 2021; out of form last summer

runners.name: Teagarden Jazz
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden yet to show anything for current yard (formerly Gordon Elliott); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden yet to show anything for current yard; hard to fancy

runners.name: Esticky End
runners.spotlight: Five-time Flat winner (last win in 2024) but yet to trouble the judge in 12 starts over hurdles; some decent runs in defeat when last seen over the winter, finished fifth of 13 at Thurles (2m, good to yielding) and fifth of 20 at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding), flying home after being hampered by a faller; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Five-time Flat winner; yet to win over hurdles but some decent runs in defeat over winter

runners.name: La Tulipe Noire
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in seven hurdle runs to date, (four of them handicaps); running moderately on Flat at the moment.
runners.comment: Unplaced in seven hurdle runs; moderate runs on Flat recently

runners.name: Boltlander
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; moderate maiden form and didn't improve for switch to handicaps in his final start for Gordon Elliott; makes stable debut now.
runners.comment: Reserve; moderate m'den form and didn't improve for switch to h'caps last time

races.race_name: Ambrex Switchgear Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Nashville Notions
runners.spotlight: Won a Sligo maiden over 2m2f last year but hasn't been near that level since; this better ground will suit but has to get back to best.
runners.comment: Won a Sligo maiden over 2m2f last year but hasn't been near that level since

runners.name: Jackmoon
runners.spotlight: Maiden showed plenty of promise in bumpers and has run some fine races over hurdles, the best of which when a close second over 2m7f at Cork; not as good over 2m4f last time but the step back up in trip can help on handicap debut; chance.
runners.comment: Not at best over 2m4f last time but the step back up in trip can help on handicap debut

runners.name: Jetaway Nana
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D mares' maiden hurdle last August; hit and miss since with Tramore last time one of her poor runs but could go well if rebounding.
runners.comment: C&D winner; poor at Tramore last time but could go well if rebounding

runners.name: Krishna Bellevue
runners.spotlight: Hints of ability in maiden hurdles last year for Ross O'Sullivan; pulled-up in a h'cap on debut for this yard; blinkers return after the cheekpieces last time.
runners.comment: Hints of ability in maidens; pulled-up in a h'cap on debut for this yard; blinkers return

runners.name: Dalmatic
runners.spotlight: First reserve; got off the mark in testing ground at Limerick last December; solid efforts on contrasting ground at Limerick and Kilbeggan last twice; squeak if getting in.
runners.comment: Solid efforts on contrasting ground at Limerick and Kilbeggan last twice; squeak; reserve

runners.name: Rhythmic Tune
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m5f Limerick h'cap on yld ground last autumn but not as good twice since in varying conditions; has to return to best; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Won a 2m5f Limerick h'cap last autumn but not as good twice since; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Aine's Lad
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles in a Thurles handicap in February over 2m7f (sft); a decent chasing debut later the same month; is 9lb higher back in this sphere on livelier ground so a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Won a Thurles handicap in February over 2m7f (sft); 9lb higher on livelier ground today

runners.name: Are You Busy
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f maiden hurdle in 2025 but has been below that form in three handicaps since; reportedly bled last time so has a bit to prove now.
runners.comment: Below form all handicap runs; reportedly bled last time so has a bit to prove now

runners.name: Johnny Cakes
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; a record of 1-21 over hurdles; decent runs on two of last three starts on contrasting ground and a shout if repeating his best.
runners.comment: Decent runs on two of last three starts and a shout if repeating his best; reserve
runners.owner: Frederick Pardy & Thomas O'Hara & Peter Dawes & Crispin Wall

runners.name: Midleton Rare
runners.spotlight: Won his third hurdle race when scoring at Bellewstown over this trip last month; was pulled-up at Kilbeggan since so has questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Won at Bellewstown but was pulled-up at Kilbeggan since so has questions to answer now

runners.name: Beggars Rock
runners.spotlight: Has been mostly chasing in the last year, winning a Kilbeggan beginners' over 2m5f; mightn't have lasted 3m2f last time and this trip back off a lower hurdles mark can suit; chance.
runners.comment: Has been mostly chasing in last year; this trip off a lower hurdles mark can suit; chance

runners.name: Watching The Clock
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a Gowran handicap over 2m last October and some decent runs in defeat after; a lesser effort when last seen over 2m7f and this trip is a question on return.
runners.comment: Won over 2m; lesser effort when last seen over 2m7f and this trip is a question on return

runners.name: Teenage Kiss
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in defeat at times up to 2m4f including at this track; has to find improvement for this step up in trip but could do.
runners.comment: Maiden has to find improvement for this step up in trip but could do

runners.name: Keep Em Quite
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in points without winning; also showed up well on hurdles debut before two lesser runs; up in trip on handicap debut; check the market.
runners.comment: Best hurdles run came on debut; up in trip on handicap debut; check the market

runners.name: Elbow Lane
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; well beaten in maidens and on h'cap debut over 2m; lots of progress needed up markedly in trip.
runners.comment: Well beaten in maidens and on h'cap debut over 2m; lots of progress needed; reserve

runners.name: Malbas
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner over 2m has been well beaten over fences and hurdles the last twice; the cheekpieces worn last time are left off; this trip is new territory.
runners.comment: Well beaten over fences and hurdles lately; the cheekpieces worn last time are left off

runners.name: Whatsavailable
runners.spotlight: His three hurdle wins have come at Fairyhouse up to this trip; has run some cracking races in defeat including third off this mark at Cork last time; a player with a repeat.
runners.comment: Was third off this mark at Cork last time; a player with a repeat

races.race_name: T.C. Cullen Memorial Novice Chase

runners.name: Supersundae
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner was runner-up in a Grade 1 novice last year before losing his form in that sphere; got off the mark on third chase start when an easy winner at Kilbeggan last time over 2m6f; penalised 6lb for that but should still be difficult to beat even dropping in trip.
runners.comment: Easy chase winner at Kilbeggan last time; penalised 6lb but should still be hard to beat

runners.name: Ma Jacks Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Gowran maiden hurdle last autumn before a fine 2nd in a Galway novice to a subsequent Grade 1 winner; hasn't gone on from that and just fair handicap runs the last twice; has work to do against some of these rivals on chase bow.
runners.comment: Just fair hurdle runs the last twice; has work to do against some of these on chase bow

runners.name: Reiki Revolution
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner took a 2m4f Punchestown maiden hurdle last autumn but was then poor twice after; his yard is in better form now but he has plenty to find on chasing debut with some of these rivals.
runners.comment: Poor last twice over hurdles; has plenty to find on chasing debut with some of these

runners.name: Workahead
runners.spotlight: Point winner was a smart maiden hurdle winner in 2024 but hasn't really built on that; the best of three runs last season was when second in a Gowran Grade 3 in February; was then outclassed in the Champion Hurdle; yard does well with chasers so can run a big race.
runners.comment: Outclassed in the Champion Hurdle; yard does well with chasers so can run a big race

runners.name: Wild Bill Hickok
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner in France last year before losing his form in the autumn; bought by current trainer for Eur52,000 and gelded since; was an easy winner on chasing debut over C&D nine days ago and can be a player today with more to come.
runners.comment: Easy winner on chasing debut over C&D 9 days ago; can be a player today with more to come

runners.name: Chosen Shant
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner but is rated only 86 over fences and is out of her depth against some of these rivals.
runners.comment: Is rated only 86 over fences and is out of her depth against some of these rivals

races.race_name: Treo Eile Handicap Chase

runners.name: Hit The Frame
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last August and not bad efforts afterwards (fell once); should strip fitter after his Down Royal return so can be a player.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last August; should strip fitter after Down Royal return so a player

runners.name: Plain Or Battered
runners.spotlight: Course hurdle winner has been running well in defeat over hurdles and fences lately; could have a bit more to offer on his eighth chase run; a player.
runners.comment: Course hurdle winner has been running well over hurdles and fences lately; a player
runners.owner: David Norris & Fergal Norris & N Dooly

runners.name: Ceroc
runners.spotlight: Is yet to win over fences but has posted plenty of good runs including here; however, he was poor over hurdles last time (also here) and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Yet to win over fences but plenty of good runs incl' here; poor over hurdles last time

runners.name: Mount Shenshan
runners.spotlight: Is yet to win over fences but posted decent runs the last twice, both at Clonmel; wasn't so good over hurdles since but is still one to consider.
runners.comment: Yet to win over fences but decent runs the last twice; not so good over hurdles since

runners.name: Tell Nobody Nothin
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m4f Kilbeggan handicap last time on similar ground; is up 6lb and has questions over this trip as he failed to see out 3m2f at Down Royal two starts back.
runners.comment: Won a 2m4f Kilbeggan handicap last time; up 6lb and questions over this trip

runners.name: Barely Made It
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden was beaten a long way here last time and is 9lb out of the handicap so makes no appeal; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced maiden was beaten a long way here last time; 9lb out of the h'cap

runners.name: Favori Logique
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner has been below his best the last twice but has run well in defeat at this track and his yard is in form so he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Below his best the last twice but has run well in defeat at this track and yard in form

runners.name: Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Surprise Down Royal winner was poor since at Downpatrick since and is 9lb out of the handicap so makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Surprise Down Royal winner was poor since at D'Patrick and 9lb out of the h'cap

runners.name: Riggs
runners.spotlight: Won this last year off 7lb lower and some decent efforts in defeat afterwards; however, he might need this first run since August.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 7lb lower; might need this first run since August
runners.owner: Paul M Butler & James Patrick Byrne

runners.name: North Of Nashville
runners.spotlight: A C&D winner in 2023; lightly-raced since; posted a decent run over shorter here last time and this trip will suit better; down 1lb; chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner in 2023; lightly-raced since; decent run over shorter here last time; chance

runners.name: Sense The Tension
runners.spotlight: Maiden in all spheres was pulled-up on return/handicap bow at Tramore last time and is 1lb out of the handicap; not a lotg of appeal.
runners.comment: Maiden in all spheres was pulled-up on return/h'cap bow at Tramore last time

runners.name: One Horse Army
runners.spotlight: Won his third chase at Fairyhouse in February before falling 4 out at Newbury next time; is up 7lb so needs more and his best form has come on softer ground.
runners.comment: Won third chase at Fairyhouse in Feb; up 7lb but his best form has come on softer

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares INH Flat Race

runners.name: The Besty Woman
runners.spotlight: Put up respectable efforts in two maiden hurdles; better form on bumper debut when didn't get a clear run under an inexperienced rider; that winner has scored again since; top amateur aboard today and looks a leading player.
runners.comment: The form of her bumper run was franked; top amateur takes over and looks a leading player

runners.name: Gortnaclea Castle
runners.spotlight: Finished a close second over 2m2f at Down Royal on debut but might not have stayed 2m4f at Clonmel since when well below that form; has to resume progress.
runners.comment: Fine debut run but might not have stayed 2m4f last time; has to resume progress
runners.owner: M O Quigley & Mrs H Lalor

runners.name: C'Est Elle
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on second point start last year and ran creditably on bumper debut at Thurles in February; should have improved from that and be a leading player once handling this faster surface.
runners.comment: Third on bumper debut at Thurles (hvy); a leading player once handling this faster surface

runners.name: Whizz Bizz
runners.spotlight: Comes from a decent dual-purpose pedigree; finished 9l behind The Besty Woman on debut at Cork but can improve plenty from that and go closer today.
runners.comment: Was 9l behind The Besty Woman on debut but can improve from that and go closer today

runners.name: Poets Call
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; by a promising young sire; dam 2m4f/2m6f hurdle winner, sister to useful 2m4f-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Daffern Seal; debutant could run respectably.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree; debutant could run respectably

runners.name: Blue Diva
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; sister to point/2m hurdle winner Tilly Toughnut; dam maiden bumpers/hurdles, half-sister to top-class hurdle/chase winner/Cheltenham Gold Cup second Sir Rembrandt; cost 43,000GBP as a 2yo then E70,000 as a 3yo; from a top local points yard; check the market.
runners.comment: Well-bred mare comes from a top local points yard; check the market

runners.name: Star Of Idaho
runners.spotlight: Twelfth foal; half-sister to winners incl' Stellar Notion (useful bumper and 2m-2m7f hurdle/chase) and Rhaegar (bumper and 3m hurdle/chase); related to some decent sorts but was a vendor buyback for only Eur4,000 as a 3yo; represents top connections and commands respect on debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred but a vendor buyback for only Eur4,000 as a 3yo; top connections so respected

runners.name: Stolen Promise
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners incl' Magical Spirit (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 109) and Prime Chief (1m2f; 77); dam unplaced but  out of US 10.5f Grade 1 winner; Flat-bred represents a local yard that can produce winners; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Flat-bred represents a local yard that can produce winners; not dismissed

runners.name: Howaya Codge
runners.spotlight: First reserve; first foal; by a useful sire; dam soundly beaten in 2m hurdles, out of 2m-2m4f hurdle winner; likely one to watch if getting a run.
runners.comment: Debutante is likely one to watch if getting a run; reserve

runners.name: Maptite Chipie
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dam 1m2f AW winner (RPR 78), half-sister to useful 2m5f/3m chase winner Glen Rocco, out of half-sister to German 1m3f Group 2 winner Network; by a useful French sire but cost only Eur2,500 as a 2yo; likely one to watch if getting a run.
runners.comment: Debutante is likely one to watch if getting a run; reserve

runners.name: Saoirses Choice
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; first foal; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 59), half-sister to 1m4f Flat and useful 2m hurdle winner Palace Rock; a dual-purpose pedigree; debutante is likely one to watch if getting a run
runners.comment: Debutante is likely one to watch if getting a run; reserve

runners.name: Our Sara
runners.spotlight: Showed some improvement on second bumper start at Bellewstown on return last month; beaten 15l then so will have to take another sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Some improvement on second bumper start at Bellewstown last month; needs more
runners.owner: Mrs Anne Gleeson/Miss Heather Heffernan/Gerard P O'Neill

runners.name: Morning Express
runners.spotlight: Late-starter was an easy winner of her second point run before a respectable fifth here on bumper debut; should build on that and run another solid race.
runners.comment: Point winner; fifth here on bumper debut; should build on that and run another solid race

runners.name: Bridgie Browne
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way on debut at Clonmel and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on debut at Clonmel and can only be watched

runners.name: Tamara Jane
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on second start in points last month in soft ground; comes from the family of some smart staying types; respected local yard; could run well.
runners.comment: Won a point last month in soft ground; decent pedigree; respected local yard; can run well
runners.owner: Whose Round Is It Now Syndicate

runners.name: Whiskey Fever
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in five point starts and beaten a long way on bumper debut so can't be fancied.
runners.comment: Failed to win in five point starts and beaten a long way on bumper debut; can't be fancied

runners.name: Green Me Granny
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off on debut at Cork six days ago and would need a transformation in that time to score; cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Tailed-off on debut at Cork six days ago and would need a transformation in that time

races.race_name: Marion Gibson Brown Memorial - Irish EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Inc') (Div I)

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame at Redcar (5f, good to firm) and Chester (5f, good), far from disgraced from a wide draw in the Lily Agnes on second occasion; David Allan's pick of Tim Easterby's three runners; should go well.
runners.comment: Takes drop in grade from Chester; David Allan's pick of Tim Easterby's three; respected

runners.name: Stripes Of Glory
runners.spotlight: Similar form at Beverley and Musselburgh, seventh of eight but not beaten far in latter; sprint-bred and looks all speed; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Signs of ability both starts; sprint-bred and looks all speed; not ruled out

runners.name: Classic Bellini
runners.spotlight: Belardo half-sister to winners Vintage Brut (5f/6f 2yo including Listed; RPR 111), Vintage Times (5f 2yo; 73) and Tequila Royale (6f; 67); dam 5f AW winner (73); worth a market check with yard running three.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the smart 2yo winner Vintage Brut; worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Dee's Funny Girl
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; Cotai Glory half-sister to Italian 6f winner Logudoro; dam unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Chicas Amigas; stable's sole 2yo runner this term has been unplaced twice.
runners.comment: 16,000gns yearling; Cotai Glory half-sister to a winning Italian sprinter

runners.name: Heffernan Kingdom
runners.spotlight: £33,000 breeze-up buy; Hello Youmzain half-sister to German 9.5f-1m4f winner Petra My Love; dam 6f-7.3f winner (RPR 96), out of 6f 2yo winner; stable just 3-45 with 2yos in last five seasons.
runners.comment: £33,000 breeze-up buy; stable just 3-45 with 2yos in last five seasons

runners.name: Inglewood
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; by Showcasing; dam 7f AW winner (RPR 83), closely related to 5f/6f winner Blue Day, out of 1m Listed winner; first 2yo runner of the season for Julie Camacho.
runners.comment: 20,000gns yearling; by Showcasing; first 2yo runner of the season for Julie Camacho

runners.name: Lady Skipworth
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns yearling; by Dark Angel; closely related to 5f-7f winner Gunnerside (RPR 84), half-sister to winners Yolo Again (5f including 2yo; 93) and Brave Nation (5f including 2yo; 93); dam 5f 2yo winner (88); looks a precocious type on pedigree and her stable has already had two 2yo winners this year.
runners.comment: Precocious type on pedigree and stable has already had two 2yo winners this year

runners.name: Pretty Little Baby
runners.spotlight: 70,000euros yearling; by Persian Force; dam 6f AW 2yo/1m turf winner (RPR 80), half-sister to 7f/1m winner Monzoon; Newmarket trainer has done well (7-42) with 2yos in his short career to date.
runners.comment: 70,000euros yearling; Newmarket trainer has done well with 2yos in short career to date

runners.name: Penny Arcade
runners.spotlight: Went off second favourite but finished last of four at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft; 7-4) five weeks ago; the winner that day followed up in the Lily Agnes at Chester and improvement seems likely.
runners.comment: Last of four behind a promising type at Musselburgh; improvement likely

runners.name: Arcadian Days
runners.spotlight: Beat only two home (one of them Bea Kindly) at Redcar (5f, good to firm; 15-2) four weeks ago; needs a big leap forward.
runners.comment: Beat only two home at Redcar four weeks ago; needs a big leap forward

runners.name: Bea Kindly
runners.spotlight: Well held at Redcar and over C&D; lots of improvement required.
runners.comment: Well held at Redcar and over C&D; lots of improvement required

runners.name: Andalnade
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always behind at Chester (50-1; wore tongue-strap) nine days ago; needs to have learned a lot in a short space of time.
runners.comment: Last at Chester on debut; needs to have learned a lot in a short space of time

races.race_name: Marion Gibson Brown Memorial - Irish EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/IRE Inc') (Div II)

runners.name: Bellatina
runners.spotlight: Shaped nicely on debut when second at Beverley (5f, good to soft) but then folded tamely when joint-favourite at Southwell (5f, AW); has obvious claims on that first run and this return to turf could be in her favour.
runners.comment: Ran poorly last time after shaping well on debut; return to turf could help

runners.name: Kodi Bear Light
runners.spotlight: Had only two opponents but still shaped well when second at Musselburgh (5f, good) 17 days ago, and the winner was a good second in the Lily Agnes at Chester next time; open to improvement and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Form of Musselburgh debut has been franked; looks the one to beat

runners.name: Golden Buzz
runners.spotlight: Invincible Army half-sister to 6f winner Badosa (RPR 62); dam 5f 2yo winner (59), out of 5f 2yo winner; sprint bred; stable runs fellow newcomer Heffernan Kingdom in the first division of this race (1.52).
runners.comment: Sprint bred; stable runs fellow newcomer Heffernan Kingdom in first division

runners.name: Miss Tuite
runners.spotlight: 15,000euros yearling; by Elzaam; dam placed five times at 6f (including 2yo; RPR 68), sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Celtic Beauty; bred to be speedy and her stable is already off the mark with its 2yos.
runners.comment: Bred to be speedy and her stable is already off the mark with its 2yos

runners.name: Callisterra
runners.spotlight: 28,000gns yearling; by Kodi Bear; dam 6f AW winner (RPR 72), sister to Group-placed 1m 2yo winner Dark Rose Angel, half-sister to 7f-1m2f winner Dark Company; stable had a first-time-out 2yo winner over C&D this month; well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Yard had first-time-out 2yo winner over C&D this month; well worth a market check

runners.name: Envision
runners.spotlight: Mohaather half-sister to winners Nearlo Of Saints (French 1m4f) and Axis (Qatari 6f dirt); dam unplaced 7f AW 2yo (RPR 60), out of 1m2f Group 3 winner; bred for longer distances in due course but still respected for capable Newmarket yard.
runners.comment: Bred for longer distances in due course but still respected for capable Newmarket yard

runners.name: Kalahari Flame
runners.spotlight: 11,000gns foal, £21,000 yearling; Sergei Prokofiev half-sister to 5.7f 2yo winner Reality Queen (RPR 60); dam prolific 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; 84), half-sister to Group-placed 5f-7f winner May Sonic; bred to be sharp and is in good hands; plausible newcomer.
runners.comment: £21,000 yearling; bred to be sharp and is in good hands; plausible newcomer

runners.name: Bettabebougie
runners.spotlight: Last at Southwell (5f) and Ripon (6f); has shown some speed and her stable won a division of this race in 2024, but she needs cheekpieces to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Last at Southwell and Ripon; needs cheekpieces to make a big difference

runners.name: Lairy Mary
runners.spotlight: Beat only one home on debut but fared much better when third of nine back at Beverley (5f, good to soft; 20-1) 12 days ago, leading for a long way; yard also runs Call Nicki; chance if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: Led for long way when third at Beverley; has a chance if taking another step forward

runners.name: Call Nicki
runners.spotlight: Showed something to build on when 7l fourth of eight over C&D (good to firm; 25-1) two weeks ago, briefly looking a threat; open to improvement and could be the stable's first string judged on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Showed something to build on when fourth over C&D two weeks ago; open to improvement

races.race_name: Metaxas Handicap

runners.name: Caraway
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden who has not finished closer than 7l to a winner but she ran into lots of trouble when 6-5 favourite for a Wolverhampton handicap (1m4f) last week; still has potential for top yard and she needs a close look back on turf.
runners.comment: 0-5 but she ran into lots of trouble when favourite at Wolverhampton latest; dangerous

runners.name: Blackisthenewblack
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt when making all in a Kempton handicap (1m4f, AW; cheekpieces added) two weeks ago; up 6lb but that was a dominant display and she's open to more progress on this switch to turf; key player.
runners.comment: Off the mark when making all for a clearcut win at Kempton; big player again up 6lb
runners.owner: Team Valor, Cayton Park Stud, A Singh

runners.name: Sweet Love
runners.spotlight: Regressive ten-race maiden who has been well held in five handicaps at up to 1m4f; continues to drop down the weights but she was always in rear at Newcastle two weeks ago and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Regressive ten-race maiden who has been well held in five handicaps at up to 1m4f

runners.name: Due To Shine
runners.spotlight: Low-level maiden who reappeared with a heavy defeat at Newcastle (1m2f, AW; 33-1) last month; that took her record to 0-6 and she's not finished close than 6l to a winner so far; opposable from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Low-level maiden who has not finished closer than 6l to a winner so far; opposable

runners.name: My Dad Frank
runners.spotlight: Struggled in his three qualifying runs and was tailed off at another big price on handicap debut at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) last month; still unexposed but he can only be watched until he shows some positive signs.
runners.comment: Has struggled at big prices in all four runs including a handicap at Beverley last month
runners.owner: S McMillan, F Reay & A D Brown

runners.name: Jambearee
runners.spotlight: Has finished tailed off in four of his five runs including in a Beverley handicap (1m2f, good to firm) last month; has lots to prove back up in trip and he's 4lb out of the weights; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Tailed off in four of his five runs including at Beverley last month; 4lb out of weights

runners.name: Raven's View
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field in all five runs including two handicaps at Newcastle (7f/1m2f) this spring; mark continues to fall but he needs to raise his game at this new trip.
runners.comment: Lightly raced gelding but he needs to raise his game at this new trip

runners.name: Gemical
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three maidens in Ireland last year and same story when upped to 1m3f on handicap/stable debut at Southwell (AW; 20-1) last month; one of two runners for yard and she needs a transformation back on turf.
runners.comment: Still unexposed but she needs a transformation back on turf; yard runs two
runners.owner: G C Gale & Stuart Brown & Diane Stewart

runners.name: Boilermaker
runners.spotlight: Brother to a fairly useful 1m4f winner; finished seventh in three qualifying runs last year and he returned after a gelding operation with a well-held fourth on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m2f, good to firm) in April; needs to find more but it's still early days and he's a possible improver on his step up to this trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding and he's a possible improver on his step up to this trip
runners.owner: W G & A G Vestey, E Gerber And P Inglett

runners.name: Faeberon
runners.spotlight: Didn't live up to market expectations in two juvenile runs and was tailed off on his reappearance here (1m2f, good to soft) in March; been gelded since and now goes handicapping but he needs a transformation on this step up to 1m4f.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer but he needs a transformation on this step up to 1m4f; been gelded

runners.name: Manbearpig
runners.spotlight: Big prices and he finished a well-held fifth in three runs on Tapeta (1m/9.4f) during the winter; now goes handicapping after a gelding operation but he needs improvement and not sure this new trip will suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Unexposed gelding but he needs improvement and not sure 1m4f will suit on pedigree

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Close fourth in a division of this contest last year but up and down since and his last two runs have been poor; dangerously well handicapped but opposable on balance.
runners.comment: Last two runs have been poor; dangerously well handicapped but opposable on balance
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Call To Action
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo; ended 2025 with a win and he showed himself better than ever when a close second of eight at Pontefract (5f, good) on reappearance; still of interest despite a 2lb rise in the weights.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; fine second at Pontefract on return; still of interest despite 2lb rise
runners.owner: D Scott, Resdev, Capla Dev & L Westwood

runners.name: Pixie Diva
runners.spotlight: Only eighth of 17 when favourite at Ascot two weeks ago, although far from disgraced; still relatively lightly raced and just 1lb higher than for 5f Newcastle win in March; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; just 1lb higher than for Newcastle win in March; not ruled out

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Placed on both his runs over C&D and he looks to be coming to the boil, beaten less than 1l when third at Musselburgh 17 days ago; blinkers are back on for the first time this season; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Looks to be coming to the boil, close third at Musselburgh last time; might be the answer

runners.name: Right And Exact
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after just four runs; consistent form in 5f novices last term and made an encouraging reappearance/handicap debut at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) five weeks ago, fading having had the worst of the draw; remains open to improvement and high on the list.
runners.comment: Unexposed after four runs; shaped well on handicap debut at Musselburgh; high on the list

runners.name: Tiriac
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never a threat both starts this year; dropped only 1lb and others make more appeal; one of three runners for Paul Midgley.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never a threat this year; one of three runners for Paul Midgley

runners.name: Emerald Harmony
runners.spotlight: Twice a 5f winner in Ireland for Johnny Murtagh, and she has made a solid start for her new yard, close third of seven at Beverley on latest outing; bubbling under and one of the likelier candidates.
runners.comment: Has made solid start for new yard; one of the likelier candidates

runners.name: Brazilian Belle
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW early this year, winning over 5f at Southwell and Wolverhampton, but she was well held behind Castan back at Southwell last time and could be vulnerable off her current mark.
runners.comment: Well held behind Castan at Southwell last time and could be vulnerable off current mark

runners.name: Law Of Average
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has won five times from 21 attempts, bouncing right back to form when making all at Bath (5f, firm) last month; back up 4lb but still well treated on 2024 form which included a C&D success.
runners.comment: Made all at Bath last month; back up 4lb but still well treated on 2024 form

runners.name: Castan
runners.spotlight: 25-1 winner of a division of this race 12 months ago; arrives in form following a success at Southwell and a creditable fourth of eight at Ripon; should be in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Won a division of this race 12 months ago and arrives in form in bid for a repeat

runners.name: Tees Aggregates
runners.spotlight: Doesn't always look the easiest of rides and he reared at the start and was slowly away prior to finishing fourth of six at Bath last Thursday; failed to beat a rival the time before; others look safer.
runners.comment: Reared at start and slowly away when fourth at Bath last Thursday; others look safer
runners.owner: Ingleby Bloodstock Ltd & The Ivy League

runners.name: Counsel
runners.spotlight: Won on AW in December (6f) and March (5f) but ran poorly at Wolverhampton four weeks ago; 0-13 on turf and others have more compelling profiles.
runners.comment: Ran poorly at Wolverhampton four weeks ago and is 0-13 on turf

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Back to his last winning mark and arrives after a close third of ten over C&D two weeks ago; runner-up in a division of this race last year; in the mix once more.
runners.comment: Back to last winning mark and arrives after close third over C&D; in the mix once more

runners.name: Glory Fighter
runners.spotlight: Admirable veteran who recorded his 11th win when making all at Nottingham (5f, good) last month; not disgraced at Ripon next time; should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Made all at Nottingham last month and not disgraced at Ripon; should make another bold bid

runners.name: Changeofmind
runners.spotlight: 0-16 since winning his first two races and beat just one home in a division of this race 12 months ago; now 8lb lower but he carries risks after seven months off.
runners.comment: 0-16 since winning first two races; down in weights but carries risks after a layoff

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: On a dangerous mark but she doesn't look in much form, last of 17 at Musselburgh on latest outing; needs to rediscover her spark.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but doesn't look in much form; needs to rediscover her spark
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, including twice over C&D; shaped as if retaining ability when eighth of ten at Nottingham (40-1) on return but may need another run to put him spot-on.
runners.comment: Probably needed reappearance at 40-1 and may require another run to put him spot-on

runners.name: Curious Rover
runners.spotlight: Second to Castan in a division of this race in 2025; led for a long way when fourth of eight at Musselburgh 17 days ago; 6lb below his last winning mark; has plenty in his favour.
runners.comment: Second here in 2025; 6lb below his last winning mark; has plenty in his favour
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

races.race_name: Sky Bet Extra Places Handicap

runners.name: Starliner
runners.spotlight: He'd taken a healthy drop in the weights during an AW spell since December and was able to take advantage at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last week; ought to have another handicap in him off this 2lb higher mark but perhaps in a less competitive race than this.
runners.comment: Took advantage of weights drop at Ayr (1m) last week; back up 2lb; this is harder
runners.owner: David Orr & Miss L A Perratt

runners.name: Military Leader
runners.spotlight: Some narrow defeats on AW over the winter but he returned from a break with his best effort yet, overcoming some interference at Southwell (1m) last week to win in good style; has a 7lb higher mark to shoulder in his first 1m turf handicap but he could prove progressive.
runners.comment: Good winner on AW (1m) last week; up 7lb; could prove progressive back on turf

runners.name: Rajapour
runners.spotlight: Has had some mixed results in 2026 but he had his path blocked when only just failing against Hale End at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) in April; travelled within himself but took an age to pick up when a never-nearer fourth at Ayr since and now has a tongue-tie added; capable of going well.
runners.comment: Mixed in 2026 but unlucky 2nd to Hale End in April; never-nearer 4th latest; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Harswell Ruby
runners.spotlight: Won a shade cosily over C&D (good) in April and may not have been best positioned on the flank when fifth of 12 behind Hale End at Wetherby (favourite) later in the month; worth another chance but this is competitive.
runners.comment: Cosy C&D winner in April; possible excuse behind Hale End at Wetherby since

runners.name: Ravishing Beauty
runners.spotlight: Had a good run of form at about 1m last summer, winning two in a row at Pontefract and Nottingham (both on good); soundly beaten on return from a winter break but may well have more to offer after that.
runners.comment: Won two in a row at about 1m (good) last summer; soundly beaten on return from a break

runners.name: Hale End
runners.spotlight: Yard won this or a division of it three times since 2016, including with this one in 2024; successful return to turf at Wetherby (1m, good to firm; second Rajapour was hampered) in April and didn't do much damage to his mark by going back up just 2lb; the stronger the pace the better; respected.
runners.comment: Won this in 2024; winning return to turf (1m) latest; back up 2lb; respected

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Much more racing on AW than turf but better than ever on grass for new yard, winning both 1m starts (good to soft and good to firm), the latest over C&D with something in hand; a further 7lb rise may not be beyond him.
runners.comment: Won both 1m turf starts for new yard, latest C&D with something in hand; up a further 7lb

runners.name: Pink Azalea
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins at about 1m on soft in 2025 but she had a quiet end to the year and was tailed off on her reappearance at Pontefract; has plenty to prove after that.
runners.comment: Three turf wins at about 1m on soft in 2025 but she's been way below form on last 3 runs

runners.name: Ey Up Its Jazz
runners.spotlight: Added two more wins in 2025, at 1m (good to soft) and on this track (7f, soft; made all); back on his last winning mark but this year's efforts lag behind what will be needed today.
runners.comment: Won at 1m and 7f in 2025 but this year's efforts lag behind what will be needed today

runners.name: Selection
runners.spotlight: Turf winner as 2yo; some good AW runs since winning at Newcastle (1m) in October but she's slacked off on her last two starts and needs a turnaround now back on this surface; has more to prove than some.
runners.comment: Turf winner as 2yo; some good AW runs since 1m win in October but below best of late

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: Both wins over 5f as a 2yo; has run some good races over 7f this spring, on AW and turf, and he kept on well enough when upped to 1m at Pontefract in April to make him worth another go at this trip; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Both wins at 5f as 2yo; has been running well and seemed to stay 1m on sole attempt

runners.name: Barley
runners.spotlight: Not badly treated on his best form at about 1m but his losing run goes back nearly two years and he's dropped right out in both runs this year.
runners.comment: Not badly treated on best 1m form but has dropped right out in both runs this year

races.race_name: Sky Bet Club Handicap

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in poor form but he built up a head of steam to take a late fourth on his recent Pontefract return (1m, good); now 4lb lower than his Haydock win (1m, good to firm) last August so weighted to be competitive.
runners.comment: Never-nearer fourth on Pontefract return; 4lb lower than 1m win in August; competitive

runners.name: New Image
runners.spotlight: Has course form; ran with great credit in stronger races than this as a 4yo when three wins included one in the Shergar Cup (first run at 1m, good to firm; acts on good to soft) at Ascot; he wasn't the same horse in either of his two runs last year but fared much better when a close third at Redcar last month; major player.
runners.comment: Excellent 4yo form at 7f/1m; out of sorts in two runs last year; back to form latest

runners.name: Capital Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Had excuses for the fizzling out of his season in 2025 but he'd been a ready winner of his sole C&D start, last May, and later won off this mark in August; appreciated the return to good to firm ground when only narrowly failing to get up at Beverley (about 7f) last month; on the premises.
runners.comment: Winner of his only C&D start, last May; later successful off this mark; close 2nd latest

runners.name: Frankies Dream
runners.spotlight: Won six races at 5f-7f between January and May 2025 but off after a lesser effort in July and down the field here on his return in April; has gone close over the extended 7f at Beverley so should stay an easy 1m if back to full strength but others look stronger for the win.
runners.comment: Won six races at 5f-7f in 2025; down the field latest after a layoff; others more likely

runners.name: Count Palatine
runners.spotlight: All four wins over 7f, once on this track; probably stays further and is worth another go over 1m but he was last to finish on his comeback here over 7f in April and likely other days.
runners.comment: All four wins at 7f; worth another go over 1m but finished last on reappearance here

runners.name: City Of Poets
runners.spotlight: Talented on AW and had more in hand than the narrow winning margin suggests when arriving late on the scene at Wolverhampton (about 1m) in November; was carried out of his ground when starting a forward move over C&D (good to firm) recently and has to be of interest in this lower grade.
runners.comment: Talented on AW; carried out of his ground over C&D latest; of interest in this lower grade

runners.name: Esherann
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won two 1m races (good) in Ireland last year; has needed both runs for this yard but stepped up on reappearance when just ahead of the hampered City Of Poets over C&D a fortnight ago; another advance is possible but needed.
runners.comment: Won two 1m races in Ireland last year; can improve on two defeats for this yard

runners.name: Empirestateofmind
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins at 1m and 1m1f (good or softer) include two over C&D; quite favourably treated now and he was a very respectable fourth at Ripon recently, when just behind a subsequent winner; needs a bit more here.
runners.comment: All wins on good or softer, two over C&D; quite well treated and fair fourth latest

runners.name: Empire Of Light
runners.spotlight: All three wins for this yard at about 1m2f on a wide range of surfaces, most recently when making a winning return to action at Haydock (made all, just hung on) in April; also effective at 1m but raised 4lb to a career-high mark; non-runner 2.55 York on Friday.
runners.comment: All three wins for yard at 1m2f, most recently on reappearance; career-high mark now

runners.name: Hot Cash
runners.spotlight: Form subsided slightly on final 2025 starts but now 2lb lower than his Carlisle win (1m, soft; acts on good) last June and he ran well for a long way in a strong Newbury handicap last month; may not be far away.
runners.comment: Form subsided slightly at the end of 2025 but 2lb below winning mark in June

runners.name: Impartiality
runners.spotlight: Winner of his first three handicaps in 2025 (up to about 1m; good) and showed this mark wasn't beyond him when going close at Glorious Goodwood (1m, heavy) last July; should come on for his recent reappearance and have a say with cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Won first 3 handicaps in 2025; good second off this mark at Glorious Goodwood; thereabouts

runners.name: Oolong Poobong
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly who put the race to bed quite convincingly for her first handicap win, at Haydock (7f, good) last May; had a much stiffer task in the Sandringham (1m, good to firm) at Royal Ascot after but ran a cracker in fourth, drifting across from a high draw; off since though so fitness unknown and market helpful; tongue tied this time.
runners.comment: Convincing winner at Haydock (7f) then cracking fourth in big 1m Ascot handicap; off since

runners.name: Zapphire
runners.spotlight: Has won on good to soft but her latest three wins, at about 1m, came on good to firm; on a highish mark now but off since September and past evidence suggests she'll come on for the run.
runners.comment: Handles most ground; on a highish mark; past evidence suggests she'll come on for the run

runners.name: Mr Professor
runners.spotlight: Has won on firm but best form on soft, as when winning the Lincoln (1m) in 2024; lightly raced with little return since but he was hampered on Newcastle AW last June; entitled to be rusty for new trainer on this first run since; missed a race earlier this month due to good to firm ground.
runners.comment: Best on soft, as when winning the Lincoln in 2024; lightly raced and lesser efforts since

races.race_name: Sky Bet Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Woodstock
runners.spotlight: On a 15-race losing run but he's well handicapped as a result and his stable is in fine form; almost certainly needed the run when seventh of 13 over C&D four weeks ago, fading; likely to build on that and more persuasive than most.
runners.comment: On a 15-race losing run but he's well handicapped; encouraging reappearance; interesting

runners.name: Mudamer
runners.spotlight: Notched two wins over this trip at Catterick last October and he has returned in fine form, making much of the running when a close third over C&D (good ground) last month; solid claims again from an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Has returned in fine form, third over C&D last time; solid claims from unchanged mark

runners.name: Glenfinnan
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for nearly two years but he's well handicapped now and he shaped much better than the result suggests when seventh of ten at Haydock (7f, good; badly hampered over 1f out) last Saturday; should go close if getting the rub of the green this time.
runners.comment: Much better than result suggests when seventh at Haydock; should go close with more luck

runners.name: Ziggy's Queen
runners.spotlight: Successful twice (6f/7f) on AW during the winter but her mark edged up and she was only seventh at Newcastle on Good Friday; used to be just as effective on turf but she needs to bounce back and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Only seventh at Newcastle on Good Friday; needs to bounce back and others appeal more

runners.name: Sergeant Wilko
