runners.spotlight: Promising reappearance after wind surgery when fourth at Newcastle (6f) but has taken two backward steps since; has his first run beyond sprint distances here and needs to bounce back after seven weeks off.
runners.comment: Two backward steps since reappearance; unproven beyond sprint distances

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: 9yo who capitalised on some leniency from the handicapper to win at Musselburgh (7f, good) three weeks ago; goes well there but he's a C&D winner too and he should be in the thick of things again from just 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Down in weights before winning at Musselburgh; in the mix again from just 2lb higher

runners.name: Patricia R
runners.spotlight: Two wins from just four attempts; comfortably beat four rivals in Carlisle novice (7f, good to firm) last August and she became upset in the preliminaries before running poorly on handicap debut here next time; retains potential and she's an interesting candidate after a layoff.
runners.comment: 2-4; had excuse on handicap debut last September; retains potential on first run since

runners.name: Sunriseontheboyne
runners.spotlight: Signed off in Ireland with Listowel win (1m, heavy) last autumn; well held at long odds in four runs (7f-1m2f) for this yard; beginning to fall in the weights but probably best watched unless strong in the market.
runners.comment: Well held at long odds for this yard; probably best watched unless strong in the market
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Sujet
runners.spotlight: Twice a winner (1m/1m1f) in Ireland for Dermot Weld; improved a bit on just second start for new connections when beaten narrowly at Chester (7f, good) last week, always in the firing line; a 3lb rise should not prevent another bold bid.
runners.comment: Close second at Chester last week; 3lb rise should not prevent another bold bid

runners.name: Brighton Boy
runners.spotlight: Won a useful Chester seller for Andrew Balding last September; not at best in two runs for current yard, although the draw did him no favours back at Chester on latest; falling in the weights and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Poorly drawn when down the field at Chester last time; falling in weights; not ruled out

runners.name: Andesite
runners.spotlight: Didn't build on successful debut for Karl Burke and he's been unplaced on all nine starts since; no immediate promise in two runs for current yard (wore cheekpieces on latest) and probably best watched for the time being.
runners.comment: Has become disappointing and probably best watched for the time being

runners.name: Perfect Part
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since winning debut; shaped quite nicely in the Spring Mile at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) on reappearance but never the slightest threat at Musselburgh next time; now tried in headgear.
runners.comment: 0-14 since winning debut; never a threat on latest start and now tried in headgear

runners.name: Westmorian
runners.spotlight: Gained three 6f wins on the AW last spring but he went off the boil and refused to race in the Ayr Silver Cup on his final outing; hard to see him breaking his turf duck after eight months off.
runners.comment: Went off the boil last term; hard to see him breaking his turf duck after eight months off

runners.name: Misunderstood
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in France as a 2yo, lost his form in 2025; weak in market and beat just one home at Newmarket on reappearance/stable debut; should come on for that run but he might need another one.
runners.comment: Ex-French; beat just one on stable debut; should come on for the run but may need another

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Handicap

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win after 22 attempts; went close at Kempton (6f) in March but his two runs since haven't been of the same standard; has shown promise on turf; has worn each of today's accessories at some point but this is the first time they have been worn together.
runners.comment: Exposed 22-race maiden; on fair mark on best form; needs new headgear combination to work

runners.name: Master Dandy
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of 5f AW handicaps in January; fully effective at 6f but he needs to leave a couple of low-key runs behind him now back on turf.
runners.comment: Two 5f AW wins in January; below par since and has a point to prove again now

runners.name: Andalprofit
runners.spotlight: There have been enough good runs since November's Redcar win to think this mark was in range and this race is much weaker than the Haydock handicap three weeks ago in which he weakened out of things only inside the final furlong; didn't look the easiest ride there but he still makes some each-way appeal back in a 0-60.
runners.comment: Capable off this mark and ran okay in stronger race at Haydock last month; each-way shout

runners.name: Ganesha
runners.spotlight: Just one win since October 2023 and that came on AW; operating off a reduced mark now though and he has been keeping on late in 5f handicaps this spring; has his first 6f start in nearly two years but a tongue-tie is now added and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark if return to 6f and/or fitting of tongue-tie has the desired effect

runners.name: Stormy Pearl
runners.spotlight: Five times a winner on turf and she lurks on a dangerous mark; ran well for second behind Eldeyaar over C&D on her reappearance last month (not the quickest away) and she did well when only fourth behind Run This Way at Redcar nine days ago having completely blown the start; should be involved if breaking on terms but that 'if' is substantial enough to prompt some caution.
runners.comment: Conditions okay, good mark, in form; big player if, and it's a big 'if', breaking on terms

runners.name: Baby Rover
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f handicap at Redcar (good to firm) last summer off 1lb lower; placed on softer than good twice after; has not hit the same heights this year but at 5f and 7f; should be at concert pitch now and a bigger run could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Of interest on 2025 best; should be at concert pitch after two runs back
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Ridgemaster
runners.spotlight: Caused a 66-1 shock when winning a 7f handicap at Newcastle on his stable debut in December; two heavy defeats (7f and 1m) have followed and he now tackles a sprint trip for the first time, with cheekpieces also given a go.
runners.comment: Shock 7f win on stable debut in December (AW); well held twice since; now has first 6f run

runners.name: Crocus Time
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D for Richard Hannon and her three runs at Thirsk for her new yard last summer yielded two close thirds and her breakthrough success; she has a 227-day absence to overcome today but is still likely to give it a good go.
runners.comment: Good C&D record last summer; 227-day absence to overcome but she should give her running

runners.name: Eldeyaar
runners.spotlight: A switch of headgear saw him end a losing run when making most in a big-field handicap over C&D last month; not in the same form when third at Southwell 16 days ago; no surprise to see him bounce back.
runners.comment: Beat 16 rivals to win a C&D handicap last month; fair third on AW since; likely to go well

runners.name: Asadjumeirah
runners.spotlight: His last turf success came over this C&D (good) but back in April 2022; this is his level now but others are more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: C&D winner; some fair AW runs this year but others appeal more for win purposes

runners.name: Solar Biricz
runners.spotlight: His second run for his new stable (5f, good to firm; visored first time) was a significant step up from his reappearance effort; returning to 6f will suit and he is one to consider.
runners.comment: Plugged on for fourth over 5f last month, his second run for new yard; can do better at 6f

runners.name: Run This Way
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; off the mark for the year with a comfortable success at Redcar nine days ago (6f, good; first win on ground without soft in the description); that win came in new blinkers which aren't guaranteed to have the same effect a second time; only 3lb higher today, though, so she has obvious claims if in the same form.
runners.comment: Comfy win in new blinkers at Redcar nine days ago; 3lb rise still leaves her competitive

runners.name: Gemini Star
runners.spotlight: Won just one of her 19 starts for Alice Haynes but it came at this track (5f, soft); her best effort in 2025 came on seasonal return (6f, good to firm) and although she ended the campaign quietly it was over 7f and 1m; sold for 1,200gns in December; intriguing runner on stable debut.
runners.comment: Just 1-19 for Alice Haynes but her win came here; good mark for stable debut; interesting

runners.name: Sanbona
runners.spotlight: Won twice in minor 5f events last summer (AW and good to soft); also ran well when second to Crocus Time over C&D last August; recent return to action at Redcar may have been needed but there wasn't enough encouragement to earn him the vote this time.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins last summer and he also ran well over C&D; low-key reappearance; others safer

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: He went desperately close at Newcastle (6f) in December off a 6lb higher mark; well beaten since and unseated his rider having reared as the stalls opened (not for the first time) at Southwell 18 days ago; capable but too risky for comfort.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but struggled in 2026 and the stalls remain an issue for him
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

runners.name: Invincible Annice
runners.spotlight: Her 5f AW win in November hasn't proved a springboard to better things; returns from 112 days off with a point to prove.
runners.comment: AW win (5f) last November but she hasn't kicked on; best watched after 112 days off

runners.name: Dream Deal
runners.spotlight: Joined Tim Easterby before a good second at Catterick on last spring's reappearance and went on to win two 6f handicaps at that track in August/September; he will need to be cherry ripe to defy this mark on his return though and the blinkers worn to victory are now replaced with cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Two Catterick wins (in blinkers) last summer; cheekpieces worn on his return; check market

races.race_name: Family Fun Day 'National Hunt' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Maskarvel
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all three Irish bumpers last year and launched hurdling career with three very encouraging runs for new stable, most recently when clear second at Aintree (2m1f, good) yesterday; big player if he makes a quick reappearance.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat at Aintree yesterday; big player if making a quick reappearance

runners.name: Mr Mojo Risin
runners.spotlight: Displayed clear promise on both hurdling starts last season, more recently when fourth at Kelso (2m, good to soft) six weeks ago, and ought to play a significant role here; tongue-tie fitted for first time.
runners.comment: Displayed clear promise on both hurdle starts last season and has big role to play here

runners.name: Saint Jeannais
runners.spotlight: Perhaps a shade disappointing when fourth of six in Ascot bumper in November but impressed when winning comfortably at Worcester (good; runner-up now a useful hurdler) on debut a few weeks earlier; looks a good prospect for novice hurdles.
runners.comment: Impressed on bumper debut in the autumn and looks a good prospect for novice hurdles

runners.name: Knolton Park
runners.spotlight: Showed nothing more than minor promise in three bumpers for David Loughnane in early part of last season and needs to take a big step forward on this stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in three bumpers in early part of last season; now with new yard
runners.owner: Mrs A Bannister-Bell & Mrs J Price

runners.name: Magna Patch
runners.spotlight: Displayed minor promise on hurdle debut at Ludlow (2m, good to soft) in February and may not have been suited by soft ground when well beaten last month but will probably be of greater interest when switched to handicaps.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability but will probably be of greater interest when switched to handicaps

runners.name: Masterfield
runners.spotlight: Well held when third of four on hurdle debut in March and fell mid-race on next outing; probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Well held when third of four on hurdle debut in March and fell mid-race on next outing

runners.name: Cappa Lass
runners.spotlight: Came second in an Irish point last spring but, following a layoff, was soundly beaten in two hurdle races last month (1m7f/2m)
runners.comment: Second in a Irish point last year but soundly beaten in first two hurdle races

runners.name: Pretty Darn Quick
runners.spotlight: In rear throughout when 250-1 for hurdling debut at Uttoxeter a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: In rear throughout when 250-1 for hurdling debut at Uttoxeter a fortnight ago

runners.name: Lilycala
runners.spotlight: Always behind (albeit saddle slipped) when 200-1 for good-ground Stratford bumper last month.
runners.comment: Always behind when 200-1 for good-ground Stratford bumper last month

runners.name: Heffalump
runners.spotlight: Out of a sister to stable's Grand National winner Ballabriggs but struggled in a soft-ground bumper in November and needs subsequent wind op to have made a big difference.
runners.comment: Struggled in soft-ground bumper in November; given wind op in December; hurdle debut

runners.name: Noble Don
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds in first two hurdle races (2m5f/2m); can't be recommended.
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds in first two hurdle races

runners.name: Anxious Moments
runners.spotlight: Struggled over C&D on last month's rules debut and dropped out of contention very tamely at Uttoxeter (2m, good) a fortnight ago; huge improvement required.
runners.comment: Struggled over C&D on rules debut and dropped out of contention tamely on next outing

runners.name: Dinkys Rose
runners.spotlight: Yet to show any significant promise after contesting a bumper and three maiden hurdles (all 2m).
runners.comment: Yet to show any significant promise after contesting a bumper and three maiden hurdles

races.race_name: Wigan Beer Company Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dream's Ka
runners.spotlight: The strength of this month's 2l Fakenham second (2m4f, good) hasn't been tested yet, but he's undeniably starting to find his level in handicaps; back up 2lb and could ideally race more sparingly than on that occasion, but among the likeliest all the same.
runners.comment: 2l second at Fakenham latest (2m4f, good); player if he can settle better this time.

runners.name: My Fortune
runners.spotlight: Handicap career yet to take off, only reviving a little for the debuted tongue-tie at Southwell latest (2m5f, good); positive response to wind surgery likelier more significant to his chances here than a 3lb ease.
runners.comment: Only minor revival in tongue-tie latest; chances rest on response to wind surgery.

runners.name: Mr Mahler
runners.spotlight: Decline in fortunes over fences last season wasn't arrested by a switch to front-running; brave to name this the day given he's 0-15 now, but new career-low mark is notably 8lb lower than when last trying hurdles.
runners.comment: Not at his best over fences when last seen; 8lb lower than for most recent hurdles run.

runners.name: Peace In The Park
runners.spotlight: Basically respectable efforts on most handicap outings so far (a lost shoe excused the one flop), doing fair late work for last month's Taunton third (2m3f, good); likelier to give his running than many.
runners.comment: Fair work late on at Taunton latest (2m3f, good); likelier to run his race than many.

runners.name: Dante's Inferno
runners.spotlight: Enough in his final two races last year (one here) suggested a return to 2m3f-2m4f is worth a go, having paid for trying to take on the winner on his previous run at such a trip; longest absent in the line-up.
runners.comment: Lacks recent match practice, but another go at today's sort of trip looks the way to go.

runners.name: Asta La Vista
runners.spotlight: Likely capable of better in handicaps than he could show at Ffos Las latest (2m, good), having refused to settle and then lost a shoe; step back up in trip appeals on pedigree, and 2lb drop is a nice bonus.
runners.comment: Excuses on recent handicap debut (2m); player dropped 2lb and stepped back up in trip.

runners.name: Greer Hill
runners.spotlight: Jumping issues less evident than before, but still didn't lay down any sort of marker on March's handicap debut after five months out (2m1f, good to soft), weakening quickly; hopes rest with the debuted tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Weakened quickly on March's handicap debut (2m1f); hopes rest with the debuted tongue-tie.

runners.name: Siege Of Acres
runners.spotlight: Early switch to chasing hasn't gone entirely to plan; looking a bit more realistic now, but needs to tease out his resources longer.
runners.comment: Returns to hurdling on a more realistic mark, but needs to tease out resources longer.

runners.name: Nevershedatear
runners.spotlight: Enters the reckoning on March's C&D mares' handicap second (good; beaten just over 1l), and race came too soon next time; 6lb rise appears steep, however, and there's potential rivalry for the lead here.
runners.comment: C&D mares' handicap second in March (good) off 6lb lower; potential pace rivalry awaits.

runners.name: Vinnie O'Neil
runners.spotlight: Yet to finish off a race especially strongly, including when a 50l last of seven on last month's C&D return (good); latest 5lb drop isn't sufficiently persuasive.
runners.comment: Faded into a 50l last of seven on recent return (C&D); down 5lb but unpersuasive.

runners.name: Vesalius
runners.spotlight: Beaten 58l when placing at Stratford last October (2m6f, good), and unseen since; no chances taken with his initial mark.
runners.comment: 58l third on most recent start in the autumn (2m6f, good); initial mark not a gift.

races.race_name: Remembering Kitty Warburton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Fasol
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 7yo who gave it a good shot from the front when third in competitive Sandown handicap (2m4f, good) last month; eased in grade here and will be fine over the shorter trip; major contender.
runners.comment: Gave it a good shot from the front when third in competitive Sandown handicap last month
runners.owner: P J Vogt And Bryan Drew

runners.name: Diamonds Galore
runners.spotlight: Clearly not herself when pulled up in valuable series final in March but quickly got career back on track with odds-on maiden win at Market Rasen (2m, good) last month; returns to a handicap on workable mark and remains lightly raced; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Odds-on maiden winner last month; returns to a handicap on workable mark; shortlisted
runners.owner: Turf Club 2024 & The Burling Family Ltd

runners.name: Paddockwood
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who finished down the field on first two British starts, in juvenile handicaps at Cheltenham Festival and Ascot in March; not beaten far on latter occasion, though, and had wind surgery soon afterwards; may yet come good for Ben Pauling.
runners.comment: Down the field on first two British starts but given wind op since; improvement possible

runners.name: City Of Diamonds
runners.spotlight: Doubled tally with ready win off today's mark at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in November but ended last season with some disappointing efforts (albeit in competitive races) and has more to prove than some.
runners.comment: Ended last season with some disappointing efforts; others have much less to prove

runners.name: Dog Fox
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete two of his three chases in the autumn and now reverts to hurdling after a break; suited by this C&D and only 1lb above his last winning mark but entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: Failed to complete two of three chases in the autumn; reverts to hurdling after a break
runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club

runners.name: Roysse
runners.spotlight: Chasing didn't go to plan last season (four attempts) and he was easily reeled in after making the running on return to hurdling in March; on a tempting mark now but his tendency to race far too freely remains a worry, and others are much less risky.
runners.comment: Chasing didn't go to plan last season and he was well beaten when back over hurdles

runners.name: Itsinthename
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who won Catterick maiden (1m7f, good to soft) on stable/hurdle debut in February; not discredited in good race on next outing and had excuse for heavy defeat last time (bled from the nose); makes handicap debut here; could still have potential.
runners.comment: Maiden winner; had excuse last time (bled); makes handicap debut; still has potential

runners.name: Krak
runners.spotlight: Made winning handicap debut in Ireland in April 2025 but had disappointing campaign for new stable last season; drops back in trip and sports first-time cheekpieces here; needs to turn a corner.
runners.comment: Had disappointing campaign for new stable last season; tried in cheekpieces here

runners.name: Fromheretoeternity
runners.spotlight: Consistent mare who kept on well for second behind progressive rival at Stratford (2m, good) last month and remains on a workable mark after subsequent 1lb nudge; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Consistent mare who kept on well for second at Stratford last month; in the mix again

races.race_name: Bangor-On-Dee Grand Wynnstay Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Obsessedwithyou
runners.spotlight: Made all in three-runner mares' chase at Wincanton (1m7f, good) last month and is now 4-8 over fences; faces tougher task from 3lb out of the weights here but is evidently in good nick, and new jockey Isabelle Ryder claims 5lb.
runners.comment: Made all in three-runner mares' chase last month and is now 4-8 over fences
runners.owner: Awr Consultancy Ltd & The Folly Partners

runners.name: Calico
runners.spotlight: Won valuable handicaps on good to soft ground at Cheltenham (2m) and Ascot (2m1f) last autumn; safely held in Grand Annual at Cheltenham Festival in March and not seen to best effect on heavy ground at Ayr last month but comes here on a workable mark and must be considered.
runners.comment: Not seen to best effect on heavy ground last month; bagged two big prizes last autumn

runners.name: Grain d'Oudairies
runners.spotlight: Safely held at last month's Aintree festival but back in calmer waters here and now back on same mark as for win at Haydock (2m4f, good to soft) in December; will be fine over this trip; might be the answer.
runners.comment: Won off this mark in December and will find this race easier than Aintree last month

runners.name: Gabriel's Getaway
runners.spotlight: Always comes with risk, given his jumping often lacks fluency and that he apparently needs to be held up in rear, but he's effectively 7lb lower than when keeping on well for second in valuable Ascot handicap (2m1f, good) in November and will be suited by this return to forecast good ground; blinkered for last two runs; cheekpieces tried today.
runners.comment: Comes with risk but he's well handicapped and will be suited by a return to good ground

runners.name: Personal Ambition
runners.spotlight: Won three-runner novice chase by 17l at Newbury (2m, soft; first-time blinkers) in February but that form is tricky to evaluate, and he was pulled up in the Grand Annual at Cheltenham in March; still needs to prove he can handle his current handicap mark and that he's fully effective on the forecast good ground.
runners.comment: Form of wide-margin novice win in February is tricky to evaluate; pulled up since

runners.name: Sir Tivo
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist who made all in small field at Wetherby (1m7f, good) on latest outing in the autumn, his 11th chase success; won when fresh last spring but is slightly out of the weights here and may find younger rivals better handicapped.
runners.comment: Signed off with good-ground win in the autumn; may find younger rivals better handicapped

runners.name: Homme Public
runners.spotlight: Well below best during five-race campaign last season and absent since pulled up on soft ground in February; had wind surgery the following month, though, and ought to be better suited by the forecast quicker conditions here; too well handicapped to rule out; swaps visor for refitted blinkers.
runners.comment: Well below best last season but had wind op in March; too well handicapped to rule out

races.race_name: Classic Additions Handicap Chase

runners.name: Special John
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite on last three starts, albeit one upped to 3m4f and Class 3 and another over hurdles; largely back on track with last month's Wincanton second, and doesn't appear weighted out of further gains.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite on last three runs, but back on track last time out; remains of interest.

runners.name: Slaney Opera
runners.spotlight: Majority of rules and point wins have been recorded around sharp left-handers, but found the fences coming too quickly even for him at Fakenham on last month's stable debut (3m5f, good; debuted cheekpieces retained here); no surprise were he to prove better than that again.
runners.comment: Fakenham's sharp track coerced him into errors latest; can prove better than that again.

runners.name: Gilbertina
runners.spotlight: Claims on March's Newcastle third (2m4f, good to soft), but cosmetically respectable form figures this year disguise one or two heavy defeats, this trip as much the issue as her jumping left at Fakenham latest.
runners.comment: Not without hope, but did jump left and didn't stay upped to 3m at Fakenham latest.

runners.name: Good Work
runners.spotlight: 1-37 and still surprisingly error-prone despite so much chasing experience, leaving his mark on several fences en route to a 8l C&D fourth latest (good); frame finish probable but hard to predict better even off this career-low mark whilst the mistakes remain; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: 1-37; another error-strewn round when fourth over C&D latest; still on a career-low mark.

runners.name: Friends Don't Ask
runners.spotlight: First form since summer 2024 when an 80-1 third at Fontwell this month (3m2f, good); trip and ground as much to suit again this time, but no certainty to back up that effort.
runners.comment: Fontwell third latest was first piece of form since summer 2024; needs to back it up.

runners.name: Cahier's Den
runners.spotlight: RPRs have been improving gently since sent handicap chasing, and a better jump at the last may have seen him oblige at Newcastle latest (3m, good to soft); won't mind good ground, having won an Irish point on it.
runners.comment: Improvement as a chaser continues gently, and went close at Newcastle latest; chance.

runners.name: Kenzo Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: Dual 3m Irish point winner (good to yielding, soft) making most; similar or same tactics haven't borne fruit in short rules career to date, but no surprise if he improves for fences as a half-brother to triple 3m chase scorer; may be worth chancing in a weak race.
runners.comment: Dual 3m Irish point winner, making most; no surprise if taking a step forward now chasing.

runners.name: Getaway Glory
runners.spotlight: Placed on both previous C&D starts, including when beaten under 1l by On The Platform in this race (good to soft; headed last); out of sorts this year, however, and needs a positive response to wind surgery.
runners.comment: Close third to On The Platform in this race in 2024; out of sorts this year, however.

runners.name: On The Platform
runners.spotlight: Mastered Getaway Glory up the run-in to land this contest off 1lb higher in 2024 (good to soft); present attainment level a matter of conjecture, having pulled up on sole outing since.
runners.comment: 1lb below the mark he defied in this race in 2024; pulled up on his sole run since.

runners.name: Walking The Walk
runners.spotlight: Conversion rate of just 2-27 over fences; several long absences following his last win in summer 2023, and hasn't been since a 47l last of five finishers in this race last year; best watched.
runners.comment: 2-27 over fences, and no wins since 2023; absent since beaten 47l in this last year.

races.race_name: Ladies Day On Saturday 23rd May Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: Made all for two good-ground novice hurdle wins last November (2m, 2m3f); lesser form this year likely more to do with a refusal to settle under restraint than the move to handicaps, but a return to previous tactics may put him in a pace battle with a few here; chasers in pedigree.
runners.comment: Made all in two novice hurdles (2m, 2m3f); pace rivalry if returning to those tactics.

runners.name: Park Annonciade
runners.spotlight: Less contested lead than often the case when making it seventh time lucky over fences at Huntingdon last month (2m, good), and Stratford second off 6lb higher since consolidated that form; up another 1lb here but pace rivalry could prove more the issue.
runners.comment: Made most at Huntingdon last month (2m, good) and backed that up latest; rivals for lead.

runners.name: Pigeon House
runners.spotlight: Can do too much in front but no alarms when making all in a Catterick claiming hurdle for Jennie Candlish in March (1m7f, good); out of a half-sister to a minor chase winner, but not bred for this job otherwise.
runners.comment: Claiming hurdle win in March (1m7f, good); didn't last out on the Flat since; chase debut.

runners.name: Is This For Real
runners.spotlight: 3-4 under Liam Harrison, gaining this year's Newcastle (good to soft) and Huntingdon (good) hurdles wins from the front but earlier bumper and point gains came under patient tactics; no chasers in immediate pedigree but his mark looks about right for this chasing/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Okay mark for chasing/handicap debut; not wholly wedded to recent assertive tactics.

runners.name: Nibras Gold
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m4f Flat winner (made all) and Summer Hurdle third last year, and jumped perfectly well when second on two of three chase starts in summer 2024; record of 62121 when returning from breaks of over 200 days.
runners.comment: Bountiful Flat/hurdles campaign last summer; two chasing seconds in 2024; goes well fresh.

runners.name: Hudson
runners.spotlight: Run of progressive hurdling RPRs halted by an unsuccessful step up to 2m7f back in January; not bred for fences and needs to prove able to translate his better work back to good ground, but trip more suitable again.
runners.comment: Trip to blame for backwards step last time; not an obvious one for chasing on pedigree.
runners.owner: Miss Jayne Brace & Mrs Karen Smith

runners.name: King Ulanda
runners.spotlight: Productive autumn included a Carlisle hurdles win (2m1f, good) off 4lb lower; two flops either side of a late fall since March, however, and doesn't enter this chasing debut in the same good heart as some.
runners.comment: Another hurdles win last autumn (2m1f, good), but two flops and a fall more recently.

races.race_name: Proudly Supporting Mental Health Awareness Week Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-54 and his last win was at Kempton (1m4f) in January; hasn't posed a serious threat in two runs for current yard but he's had traffic issues on both occasions and was beaten less than 2l at Southwell (1m3f) last time; on same mark here and he's a big player if things go more to plan.
runners.comment: Didn't get much luck when third at Southwell latest and he's an interesting contender

runners.name: Recobella
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent sort with record of 1-16; finished behind Sisterandbrother when fifth at Southwell (1m3f) last month but a close third in big field over C&D on her reappearance in March; has claims if she can recapture that form and her win was on good ground.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but looks interesting on her close third over C&D in March; dangerous

runners.name: Piecederesistance
runners.spotlight: His last win was in July 2022 but he returned to the Flat for new yard with a front-running third at Pontefract (1m2f, good; tongue-tie added) two weeks ago; that was an encouraging effort after a break and he has possibilities if he can build on that.
runners.comment: On long losing run but he ran well for a long way back on the Flat last month; in the mix

runners.name: It's Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last April but he's 0-9 since and has been well held in four runs this year; now 10lb lower than for his win but he weakened quickly at Nottingham three weeks ago and others are preferred; cheekpieces (tried once last year) are reapplied.
runners.comment: Just one win from 13 starts and he's been well held in last eight runs; others preferred

runners.name: In The Post
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 323 in maiden/novice events but he's been well held in four handicaps; may have had stamina issues over 2m at Lingfield (AW) last month but he needs to raise his game on this big drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in four handicaps and he needs to raise his game on this big drop back in trip

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Just one placed effort from 30 starts and and he's struggled in three runs for new yard this spring; has lots to prove and he was withdrawn after bolting before the start at Beverley 12 days ago; stablemate of Recobella.
runners.comment: Just one placed effort from 30 starts and things have not gone well this spring

runners.name: Cavalry Call
runners.spotlight: Won at Chelmsford (1m2f, AW) last spring but he's struggled in his last eight starts including over hurdles in last two runs; now 10lb lower than for his last success but he needs a big turnaround for new yard (has changed hands and left Mike Smith); hood removed and cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Has struggled under both codes for his current yard and needs a big turnaround

races.race_name: John Parker's Big 50th Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Noble Raider
runners.spotlight: Needed the experience when a clear second on Tapeta debut and improved plenty when third to a better-fancied stablemate at Newmarket a fortnight ago; clearly sets the standard for a trainer who's 2-6 with 2yos here.
runners.comment: Third to better-fancied stablemate at Newmarket a fortnight ago; clearly sets the standard

runners.name: Enemy Lines
runners.spotlight: Trainer is a surprising 0-29 wth 2yos here but had plenty placed (0-6 with them this spring); related to winners but not as juveniles; 5,000euros yearling who was sold for 1,600gns as 2yo; the market will guide.
runners.comment: Related to winners; sold for 1,600gns as a 2yo; the market will guide

runners.name: Social Spirit
runners.spotlight: Gelded 22,000gns yearling whose half-sister won over 7f as a 2yo (dam stayed middle distances); may ultimately want more of a stamina test.
runners.comment: Half-sister won over 7f as a 2yo; may ultimately want more of a stamina test

runners.name: Dancingonthestars
runners.spotlight: Trainer won this with favourite Kamakameleon last year (second time out; proved useful); this early-foaled 40,000gns half-brother to three winners, including one on 2yo debut, holds sales-race entries later on and is a likely type on paper.
runners.comment: Trainer won this last year; holds a few sales-race entries later on; likely type on paper

runners.name: El Floridita
runners.spotlight: Trainer saddled a rare early 2yo winner recently (second time out; 4% strike-rate with them on turf in recent years); this £50,000 first foal of a French maiden would be an even rarer first-time-out juvenile winner.
runners.comment: Trainer had a rare early 2yo winner recently; this £50,000 newcomer is likely best watched
runners.owner: M Scaife, S Perkins, Miss A & O Marnane

runners.name: Wolf's Gallop
runners.spotlight: £26,000 yearling, £42,000 breeze-up 2yo who's a rare juvenile runner for his jumps-oriented yard; dam won twice as a 2yo for Ralph Beckett; worth a look in the market.
runners.comment: £26,000 yearling, £42,000 breeze-up 2yo; rare juvenile runner for his yard; market useful

runners.name: Wopbopaloomop
runners.spotlight: Disappointing effort as favourite on Redcar debut and didn't fare any better last time (Tapeta), when going hard enough in front in a first-time tongue-tie; needs a deal more now taking on the boys for the first time.
runners.comment: Disappointing effort as favourite on debut and didn't fare any better last time (Tapeta)

runners.name: Everatease
runners.spotlight: Market drifter when well held on his Tapeta debut; a better effort at a big price at Leicester last time gave hope but he'll need another decent step forward if he's to take this.
runners.comment: Better effort at a big price at Leicester last time; will need another decent step forward

runners.name: Alpha Legend
runners.spotlight: Stamina in his pedigree; among the favourites for a 5f Leicester maiden on debut three weeks ago but looked to need the experience (behind Everatease) and met a bit of traffic late; ought to do better now he's had a run.
runners.comment: Stamina in pedigree; among the favourites on debut; ought to do better now he's had a run
runners.owner: Heart Of The South Racing 146 & Partner

races.race_name: Racing Welfare Supporting Racing's Workforce Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Untamed
runners.spotlight: Clear second to the favourite on his Polytrack debut in March and built on that when easily winning an ordinary-looking Ripon novice (6f, good to soft) a month ago; open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Easily won an ordinary-looking Ripon novice a month ago; open to further improvement

runners.name: Flight Control
runners.spotlight: 200,000euros yearling who was sold by Coolmore for 8,000gns last October; started a huge price but created a good impression when stringing out his rivals in C&D novice recently; holds leading claims under a penalty.
runners.comment: Started a huge price but created a good impression on C&D debut; holds leading claims

runners.name: Tip Foster
runners.spotlight: Weak 18-1 shot for his Pontefract debut (good to soft) but ran well for a long way behind the two market leaders; since withdrawn on account of unsuitable (good) ground; entitled to improve.
runners.comment: Ran well for a long way behind the two market leaders on debut; entitled to improve

runners.name: You Mystify Me
runners.spotlight: Some way off the front pair on her debut here three weeks ago and didn't improve much when second to a long odds-on shot next time (both 5f); gets the sex allowance under 7lb claimer Lewis Chalkley, though, which gives her each-way claims.
runners.comment: Gets the sex allowance under 7lb claimer Lewis Chalkley, which gives her each-way claims
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29 & South Bank Racing

runners.name: Jellystone Park
runners.spotlight: Very well backed ahead of his Polytrack debut in March, when shaping fairly well; that effort has been well advertised by the front pair since and he's entitled to improve; one to consider.
runners.comment: Very well backed on debut; that effort has been well advertised by the front pair

runners.name: Blue Icon
runners.spotlight: Gelded third foal of a mare who won twice over this trip on the AW as a 2yo; the market will be the ultimate guide on debut for a yard also responsible for Elias Escape.
runners.comment: Market will be the ultimate guide on debut for a yard also responsible for Elias Escape

runners.name: Brash
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros foal; gelded half-brother to two Tapeta winners, including one on 3yo debut, and the stable's three-time 5f turf winner Profitable Edge; market long been a useful guide with newcomers from this yard (also saddle Tip Foster).
runners.comment: Market long been a useful guide with newcomers from this yard (also saddle Tip Foster)

runners.name: Elias Escape
runners.spotlight: £40,000 breeze-up 2yo who's a half-brother to several winners; went off a big price when finishing last of five on his 7f debut recently; Oisin Orr rides Blue Icon.
runners.comment: Big price when finishing last of five on his 7f debut recently; Oisin Orr rides Blue Icon

runners.name: Frio
runners.spotlight: Eyecatching third on his debut for the Quinn yard last spring before hanging left when beaten at a short price at York a month later (front pair proved useful in both races); absent for a year since and the market will be the best guide for his new trainer.
runners.comment: Promising 2yo; absent for a year and market will be the best guide for his new trainer

runners.name: Elite Approach
runners.spotlight: Son of a 7f winner; came home nicely on his 6f Tapeta debut late last month (some way off the front three); worth keeping an eye on but maybe with a view to handicaps in due course; market useful.
runners.comment: Worth keeping an eye on but maybe with a view to handicaps in due course; market useful

runners.name: Auradue
runners.spotlight: 50-1 for her belated debut over 1m here a fortnight ago, when finishing a remote last; likely she's one for low-grade handicaps down the line.
runners.comment: Remote last on belated debut; likely she's one for low-grade handicaps down the line

runners.name: Kilfrush Blue
runners.spotlight: Single-figure price for Newbury debut (6.5f) for Jack Channon last August but ran poorly and has since been gelded; changed hands for 5,000gns in February; likely best watched unless there's good support.
runners.comment: Sold for 5,000gns in February; likely best watched unless there's good support
runners.owner: G P Racing And Mrs R Carr

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Novello Lad
runners.spotlight: Started favourite on his first nine starts last year, winning two of them, both off lower marks; hit traffic over an inadequate 5f on his comeback a few weeks ago, which will ensure he's popular once again for a yard also responsible for Cairdeas.
runners.comment: Hit traffic over an inadequate 5f on his comeback and will rightly be popular once more

runners.name: Supreme King
runners.spotlight: C&D winner two years ago who tends to stick to the AW these days; back up in the weights following a hat-trick in the winter and needs to bounce back from a lesser effort when last seen five weeks ago.
runners.comment: Tends to stick to the AW now; back up in the weights following a hat-trick in the winter

runners.name: Lord Bertie
runners.spotlight: Four wins have come on good to soft/soft ground later in the year and he wants a good test over this trip on good ground; turned in a solid comeback effort over C&D in March but there's little room for error off this mark and he has his quirks.
runners.comment: Four wins have come on good to soft/soft ground later in the year; high in the weights

runners.name: Wreck It Ryley
runners.spotlight: Handicapper hit him hard after he made it 2-2 at Leicester off 7lb lower last month and he's struggled in two runs since; hit and miss now and, while it's possible he'll bounce back, others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Hit and miss these days; possible he'll bounce back but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Cairdeas
runners.spotlight: Regressed for William Haggas before sold last summer; the handicapper is easing off so he should be easy enough to place in due course but he's done a lot of his racing over 7f and now returns from an absence; Novello Lad looks his trainer's best shout.
runners.comment: Done a lot of his racing over 7f and Novello Lad looks his trainer's best shout

runners.name: Havana Rum
runners.spotlight: String of solid efforts for Ethan Tindall, including two wins, in the past nine months; on a career-high mark, having been nudged up another 2lb, but there was nothing wrong with his comeback effort at Pontefract early last month (5f); has to be considered.
runners.comment: String of solid efforts for Ethan Tindall in the past nine months; has to be considered

runners.name: Noble Consort
runners.spotlight: Has done nothing but progress since the visor went on, winning five of his last eight starts (including C&D) and going up 31lb in the weights; off for eight months since winning at Goodwood in September and will need another career-best off this mark.
runners.comment: Done nothing but progress in the visor; needs another career-best off this mark on return
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: HK Fourteen
runners.spotlight: Proven winner who found another burst of improvement for cheekpieces going on last autumn; generally kept to 5f and no headgear as he returns from six months off; also has to prove this sort of mark is within range.
runners.comment: Generally been kept to 5f and has no headgear on as he returns from six months off

runners.name: Lorna B
runners.spotlight: Won her first two starts in handicaps last summer and had excuses off her revised mark latterly; a surprise maiden win on comeback last spring gives hope as she returns from eight months off and she remains low mileage.
runners.comment: Won her first two starts in handicaps last summer and had excuses latterly; considered

runners.name: Men Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Maiden who was picked up for 12,000gns after a fair effort in a heavy-ground Irish handicap (7f) last autumn; no surprise were he to need this after seven months off and would want to see some market interest before adding him into the mix.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; no surprise were he to need this after seven months off; watch the market
runners.owner: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Gallant
runners.spotlight: Wins for Andrew Balding came on Polytrack, latterly off this mark over 1m last summer; a slow start did for him down to 5f last time and, while he doesn't always look the easiest and can lug left, he's capable if things fall his way.
runners.comment: Slow start did for him down to 5f last time; not easy but capable if things fall his way

runners.name: Coachello
runners.spotlight: Long time since he won on turf and isn't as good now but remains consistent; returns from three months off but he can go well fresh; down to a career-low mark but he'd have preferred plenty of rain.
runners.comment: Can go well fresh and is down to a career-low mark but he'd have preferred plenty of rain

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Watchdog
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the drop in class back on turf when well supported at Redcar last month (6f, good to firm; made all); up 4lb and this is stronger but he enters the equation.
runners.comment: Well backed when 6f winner last time; this is stronger upped 4lb but enters the equation
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Black Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Consistent since handicapping and is back around the mark off which he won a Nottingham nursery last August; hit traffic on the return to 6f last time and is one of the likelier winners.
runners.comment: Consistent; hit traffic on return to 6f last time and is one of the likelier winners

runners.name: Slot
runners.spotlight: Three fair efforts on Tapeta to date (dam won six for the yard on that surface, five over 5f), including from the front on comeback four weeks ago; will need improvement if she's to win off this mark on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Three fair Tapeta runs; needs improvement if she's to win on her turf debut off this mark

runners.name: Wise Crack
runners.spotlight: Best effort for John Ryan (from whom he was picked up for 10,000gns) last year came on the AW; poor debut effort for his new yard a month ago and needs to improve a lot on that.
runners.comment: Poor debut effort for his new yard a month ago and needs to improve a lot on that

runners.name: Orange Emperor
runners.spotlight: Again inclined to pull hard, despite the hood, on his first crack at 7f last time; the return to sprinting should suit on just his second turf start.
runners.comment: Return to sprinting should suit (pulled hard over 7f latest) on just his second turf start

runners.name: Believeinmenow
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who was always well positioned from a rail draw when going close off 3lb lower at Beverley 12 days ago; needs more on just his second go beyond 5f.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden who was always well positioned last time; needs more over 6f

runners.name: Milford Grange
runners.spotlight: Bombed when among the favourites on comeback and is best judged on his Ripon second a month ago; hard to know the worth of that, however, with him finishing well held by the favourite having led against the rail; market useful.
runners.comment: Hard to know the worth of Ripon second, with him leading against the rail; market useful

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: Rain-softened ground may have been behind a poor comeback effort, following wind surgery, and he duly overcame traffic issues when winning off 4lb lower at Windsor (5f, good to firm) 12 days ago; could well have more to offer.
runners.comment: Overcame traffic when winning off 4lb lower at Windsor (5f); could well have more to offer

runners.name: Concert
runners.spotlight: Ex-Haggas filly who was said to have been unsuited by rain-softened ground when running well below market expectations back on turf last time (behind Coyy); should do better.
runners.comment: Said to have been unsuited by rain-softened ground last time; should do better

runners.name: Uncertainty
runners.spotlight: Didn't progress for Richard Hannon prior to being sold for £13,500 last autumn; said to have hung right when last of four on her debut for this yard (1m, Tapeta) and is hard to fancy back from six months off.
runners.comment: Didn't progress for Richard Hannon before sold; hard to fancy back from six months off

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap

runners.name: Masaban
runners.spotlight: Needed the experience in two runs for Dermot Weld last summer before changing hands for 20,000euros; started odds-on for his handicap debut eight days ago, when winning easily from the front, and holds obvious claims despite an 8lb rise.
runners.comment: Won easily from the front last time and holds obvious claims despite an 8lb rise

runners.name: Tarmonbarry Kid
runners.spotlight: Didn't go on last season and was gelded; only beat one home on his C&D comeback three weeks ago but shaped well for a long way and is entitled to have come on for that; worth a second look down 2lb.
runners.comment: Shaped well for a long way on his C&D comeback; worth a second look down 2lb

runners.name: Palace Artois
runners.spotlight: Ran well below market expectations on her AW comeback and again well held from another wide stall last time (overraced in first-time blinkers); powerful yard in top gear now after a quiet early spring and she starts handicap life at a lowly level.
runners.comment: Well below market expectations on her AW comeback; starts handicap life at a lowly level

runners.name: Smartanck
runners.spotlight: Dual Tapeta winner, including at 50-1 in March on his first start since wind surgery; tailed off on turf at the same price early last month, since when he's been gelded; a shock to see him take this.
runners.comment: Tailed off early last month, since when he's been gelded; shock to see him take this

runners.name: High Chieftain
runners.spotlight: Trainer going well of late and boasts a fair record here; this gelding went the wrong way on the AW in the winter and will need to improve for first-time headgear on his turf debut back from a break.
runners.comment: Trainer going well and has a fair record here; this one needs to improve for headgear

runners.name: Irish Incentive
runners.spotlight: This maiden is more exposed than some and not convincing on the stamina front in a couple of tries over 1m; also needs to improve on recent AW efforts.
runners.comment: More exposed than some; not convincing on the stamina front in a couple of tries over 1m

runners.name: Haltonwood
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in three runs to date, playing up at the start on his turf debut four weeks ago; interesting to see how he goes in the market now handicapping in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Interesting to see how he goes in the market now handicapping in a first-time tongue-tie

runners.name: Ice Cube
runners.spotlight: Started a short price on his comeback/handicap debut late last month, having been gelded, when getting involved in a dual up front and cracking late; trainer going well of late and this one's evidently felt capable off this mark.
runners.comment: Started a short price on his comeback; evidently felt capable off this mark; shortlisted

runners.name: Balmoral Boy
runners.spotlight: Well-related gelding who's finished well held in three runs at big prices, latterly when last of five over 7f here three weeks ago; this is more realistic but would want to see some support before considering him.
runners.comment: This is more realistic but would want to see some support before considering him

runners.name: Crimson Sunset
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in three runs to date and starts handicap life at a sensible level; room for improvement at the stalls and she'll want to settle better than in the past from a wide stall but is one to consider.
runners.comment: Signs of ability in three runs to date and starts handicap life at a sensible level

runners.name: Princess Coco
runners.spotlight: String of poor efforts to date, always at big prices; first-time blinkers will need to make a massive difference from stall 16.
runners.comment: String of poor efforts to date; first-time blinkers will need to make a massive difference

runners.name: Gloriola
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden whose latest effort in a Wetherby 0-55 didn't amount to much; would be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden whose latest effort in a Wetherby 0-55 didn't amount to much

runners.name: Mayflower Billy
runners.spotlight: Hasn't achieved much in three runs at big prices; pulled hard upped to 1m2f last time, having appeared flattered at Thirsk, and it's easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Not achieved much in three runs at big prices; easy enough to look elsewhere

runners.name: Red Rifle
runners.spotlight: Drawn away from the action on his nursery debut last backend and pulled hard upped to 1m2f on his comeback; likely he's capable of better but he was well held each time and is more exposed than some.
runners.comment: Likely he's capable of better but he is more exposed than some; only holds outside claims

runners.name: Raise The Stakes
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a few fair winners; has been gelded and goes in first-time headgear on his handicap debut; didn't offer much in three runs last backend but is worth tracking in the market for a trainer who won this in 2024 (only 2-63 here all told though).
runners.comment: Half-brother to a few winners; been gelded and goes in first-time headgear; market useful

runners.name: New Objective
runners.spotlight: Finished some way off a couple of well-handicapped rivals on his nursery debut (Tapeta, later placed second) for Jack Morland when last seen five months ago; will have to improve on that if he's to overcome a wide draw for his new yard.
runners.comment: Will have to improve on 2yo work if he's to overcome a wide draw for his new yard

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Dion Baker
runners.spotlight: 0-30 on AW but he's won four times on turf (7f); struggled during the winter but he returned to this scene with a respectable effort at Yarmouth (6f, good) last month; should benefit from that run and he's in the mix on this step back up in trip; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on turf (7f) and he's in the mix on this step back up in trip

runners.name: Typeface
runners.spotlight: Made it 2-4 on turf with an emphatic win at Ffos Las (1m, good) last August; ended last year with a major blip at Newcastle but that was after two seconds at that track; regular tongue-tie is missing on his return but he still looks on a workable mark and should get a good pace to aim at on this drop to 7f; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Record of 2-5 on turf and he needs watching in the market on return; tongue-tie is missing

runners.name: Shark Two One
runners.spotlight: All five wins have been on turf, the last three over this trip; only 2lb higher than for his latest success (June 2025) and he reappeared with a promising effort over 6f at Leicester (good to firm) last month; looks interesting on this step back up in trip and he's versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on Leicester reappearance and he looks interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: Seven of his nine wins have been on AW but he's well handicapped on some runner-up efforts on turf last season; has mixed record since his last success 12 months ago but he was placed at Southwell (1m) last time and has claims if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Returned to form with third at Southwell last month and has claims if he can build on that

runners.name: Moonhall Lass
runners.spotlight: 0-22 in Ireland but has form figures of 311 since joining James Owen and she stayed on strongly when scoring by 3l in an amateur jockeys' handicap at Salisbury (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago; back up 8lb but she remains well handicapped on old form and is effective at this trip; key player.
runners.comment: 0-22 in Ireland but has form figures of 311 since joining James Owen; key player

runners.name: Al Baahy
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won classified events at Wolverhampton (9.4f) and Lingfield (1m2f) in January but he's found things tough back in handicaps in his last three runs; has something to prove again and his sole turf win was in May 2023.
runners.comment: 7yo who has been well held in his last three runs and sole turf win was in May 2023

runners.name: Heretic
runners.spotlight: All 27 runs have been on AW; back-to-back wins at Southwell (1m) last October but he's been up and down since and was beaten 12l at Southwell two weeks ago; now makes his belated turf debut and comes with risks attached.
runners.comment: All 27 runs have been on AW and he comes with risks attached on belated turf debut

runners.name: Starshot
runners.spotlight: Just one win from 43 starts but he's reached the frame on AW at Southwell (1m/7f) in his last two runs; usually comes from off the pace and has claims if he transfers his recent form back to turf.
runners.comment: Overall record of 1-43 but he's run some good races on AW this spring; not ruled out

runners.name: Thanks Dad
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been gained at Southwell (6f/7f) but he was out of sorts there in his final three runs last year; on lower mark back on turf but he's 0-8 in this sphere and has something to prove after five months off.
runners.comment: On lower mark back on turf but he's 0-8 in this sphere and has plenty to prove on return

runners.name: Flying Star
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who has form figures of 044487577 in handicaps; has dropped down the weights but slow starts have been a recurring theme and she never got involved on Redcar reappearance (7f, good to firm) last month; down the list.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden and slow starts have been a recurring theme; others preferred

runners.name: Fifty Sent
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when winning two classified events at Southwell (1m) in March but he's been well held back in handicaps in his last two runs; didn't run badly at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month but that took his turf record to 1-18 and he has work to do back at this trip.
runners.comment: Record of 1-18 on turf and was a well-held fourth at Nottingham latest; down the list

runners.name: Bullington Bry
runners.spotlight: 19-race maiden; had a near-miss at Ffos Las (7.3f, good) last summer and was a fair fourth at Southwell (7f, AW) last week; seems tactically versatile and has each-way claims if he responds well to different headgear.
runners.comment: 0-19 but in the frame at Southwell last week and could go well in different headgear

runners.name: Oselton
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough with a clearcut success at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) last September but he's not gone on from that and was down the field on Thirsk reappearance (7f, good) last month; still 8lb higher than for his win and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Best form has been on AW; sole win was off 8lb lower at Southwell; others preferred

runners.name: Ziva's Star
runners.spotlight: 0-19 but the odd run has hinted at better and she's only been beaten around 3l in two runs here (5f/6f, good to firm) this season; looks worth another try at this trip and she could get involved.
runners.comment: 0-19 but she hasn't been beaten far in two runs here this season; could go well

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: 13-time winner but last five have been at Newcastle and his last turf win was almost three years ago; on a dangerous mark and he wasn't beaten far at Ayr (7f, good to firm) 11 days ago but this is a much deeper race and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: 12yo who wasn't beaten far at Ayr latest but his last turf win was almost three years ago

runners.name: Carida
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in six attempts; slipping towards a basement mark as a result of that but needs to show more before she can be recommended.
runners.comment: Unplaced in six attempts and needs to raise her game to get involved

runners.name: Rocket Rod
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner who is 1-13 on turf; well treated on old form but he's returned from almost two years off with two heavy defeats at Newcastle (7f/1m) this spring; hasn't worn headgear since 2021 and he needs a major revival with cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Returned after long absence with two heavy defeats on AW this spring; cheekpieces back on

runners.name: Lowestoft
runners.spotlight: Didn't show a great deal in four runs for Christine Dunnett and was ninth of ten on handicap/stable debut at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in January; tongue-tie is now added but he needs a transformation after a break.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he needs a transformation for new yard; tongue-tie added

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Tilsworth Max
runners.spotlight: Plenty of good efforts on AW during the winter, including a win at Lingfield (7f); still looks feasibly treated on that form but he didn't shine back on turf at Nottingham (8.3f, good) last month and that took his overall record to 1-28; mixed messages.
runners.comment: In good form on AW during the winter but he didn't shine back on turf last month

runners.name: No Gain
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 13 runs was in Jersey (1m, firm) last summer; made a promising start for new yard when fourth at Wolverhampton (6f) in March but she's taken two backward steps since; bit to prove but this step back up in trip is a positive and she could go well if she gets the breaks.
runners.comment: Below best on last two starts but this step back up in trip is a positive; could go well

runners.name: Zu Run
runners.spotlight: His last win was at Brighton (7f, goof to firm) last May but that was off 11lb higher and he's shown signs of a revival on Tapeta recently; should get a good tow into this race and could be dangerous back on turf.
runners.comment: Now 11lb lower than for his last win and he's shown signs of a revival recently; dangerous

runners.name: Lion Ring
runners.spotlight: Won two classified events at Wolverhampton (5f/6f) this year but he's not made an impact in last three runs; this step back up to 7f is no problem but he needs to find more to get back on the scoresheet.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year but he needs to find more to get back on the scoresheet

runners.name: War Memorial
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the 20th attempt when battling well to justify support at Musselburgh (7f, good) two weeks ago; up 4lb but he beat a clear second last time and his confidence could be boosted after that success; big player again with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Off the mark when beating a clear second at Musselburgh; big player again up 4lb

runners.name: Sedgemoor
runners.spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he's won twice at Newcastle (6f, AW) this year and bounced back with a creditable third at Haydock (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; that was an eye-catching effort from off the pace and he's respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Two AW wins this year and placed at Haydock last time; respected back up in trip

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but he's finished runner-up at Southwell (7f) in two of his last three runs, including a bold bid last Thursday; only 1lb higher than for that close call and he's a big player if he can transfer his form back to turf.
runners.comment: Went close at Southwell last week and he's only 1lb higher here; respected

runners.name: Melissa Honey
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden but she's on a reduced mark and was a fair fourth at Southwell (6f, AW) 18 days ago; this step back up in trip should be fine and she could be in the mix.
runners.comment: 0-13 but a fair fourth on AW latest and she could be in the mix back up in trip

runners.name: Create
runners.spotlight: Blows hot and cold and she's struggled on Tapeta this spring in her three runs after a break; plenty to prove and probably more potent at 1m these days.
runners.comment: Blows hot and cold and she's struggled since returning from a break this spring; opposable
runners.owner: Dixon, Wylam, M Baldry, J&s Harrod

runners.name: Eulalia
runners.spotlight: 4yo who had five runs for Charlie Fellowes last year and she found improvement when a strong-finishing third in a Kempton handicap (7f) in October; looks interesting on that form but that is her standout effort and she failed to beat a rival on stable debut at Kempton (1m; tongue-tie added) in March; comes with risks attached after another break.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly but she's been well held in most of her runs; hard to weigh up

runners.name: Diligent Henry
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner but he's been off the boil on Polytrack and Tapeta since December; still lightly raced on turf but he needs to rediscover his spark back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but he's been off the boil in that sphere since December; others preferred

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but he's on a dangerous mark and has reached the frame at Nottingham (8.3f, good) and Beverley (8.4f, good to firm) in his last two starts; now drops back in trip but he should get a good pace to aim at and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Has reached the frame in his last two starts and he's not ruled out back in trip

runners.name: Albeyours
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Leicester (7f, good to soft) last September and ended last season with two solid efforts over this trip; returns after 211 days off but she still has low mileage and needs watching in the market for in-form yard.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester last autumn and she still has low mileage; needs a close look on return

runners.name: Summer Rain
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who finished out the back on turf (1m/5f, good) in his final two runs last summer; returns for new yard (left Stan Moore) after 311 days off and has plenty to prove back up in trip; blinkers worn last time are removed.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden and he has plenty to prove on comeback for new yard

runners.name: Mini Mac
runners.spotlight: One win apiece for his previous two yards but he looks far from solid on 2026 form for another new stable; best watched unless the betting suggests a big run is likely; cheekpieces are removed and blinkers return.
runners.comment: One win apiece for his previous two yards but looks far from solid on 2026 form

runners.name: Kitaab
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner but he's generally struggled this year and was beaten 10l at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) last month; continues to drop down the weights but he has plenty to prove and is 0-11 on turf.
runners.comment: Has generally struggled this year and this dual AW winner is 0-11 on turf; passed over

runners.name: Piddie's Pearl
runners.spotlight: Has struggled at 20-1 or bigger in all five runs, including two AW handicaps (7f) in September; still early days but she needs a transformation on her return.
runners.comment: Has struggled in all five runs and she needs a transformation after 236 days off

races.race_name: QuinnBet Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Chase (Arc Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: King Of Cong
runners.spotlight: Still to score over fences and has been pulled up in 3m handicap chases at Hexham on his last two runs; tried in different headgear.
runners.comment: Still to score over fences and pulled up in handicap chases at Hexham on his last two runs

runners.name: King Of The Story
runners.spotlight: Scored three times (at 2m4f) over fences last summer; let down by his jumping after but back on track with fourth of 12 at Fontwell (2m3f) 22 days ago; not out of things.
runners.comment: Back on track with fourth of 12 at Fontwell (2m3f) 22 days ago; not out of things

runners.name: Independent Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Bangor (2m4f) in September; placed twice when sent chasing after but only sixth in 2m handicap at Musselburgh when last seen in November; could bounce back after a break though.
runners.comment: Placed in first two chases; below that form in November; needs to bounce back after break

runners.name: Jiair Madrik
runners.spotlight: Fair ex-French hurdler/chaser but yet to match that form for his current yard, undergoing breathing surgery before a well-held fourth of six in 2m1f handicap chase at Market Rasen last month; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth of six in handicap chase at Market Rasen last month; others appeal more

runners.name: Annie Nail
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Fakenham (2m7f) in April but she made mistakes when a remote third of five in 2m5f handicap chase there 12 days ago; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Scored at Fakenham in April but she made mistakes when a remote third since

runners.name: Universal Secret
runners.spotlight: In excellent form over fences since undergoing a second breathing operation, scoring at Wincanton (2m4f) in February before a clear second of seven over that C&D last month; up 2lb but that form is working out well so he's a big player.
runners.comment: Won at Wincanton before clear second there last month; up 2lb but form franked; big player

runners.name: Quickasican
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 9yo who was pulled up in 2m4f handicap chase at Hexham two weeks ago; not proving easy to catch right.
runners.comment: Pulled up in 2m4f handicap chase at Hexham two weeks ago; not proving easy to catch right

runners.name: Ironica De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 8yo who has been pulled up on his last three outings, cheekpieces tried in 2m Warwick handicap chase 23 days ago; hard to warm to.
runners.comment: Pulled up on last three outings, cheekpieces tried at Warwick latest; hard to warm to

runners.name: Rockvale
runners.spotlight: Placed in points but yet to make the frame in six starts under rules, a well-held sixth in 3m handicap chase at Bangor last month; easy to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Yet to make the frame in six starts under rules, sixth at Bangor last month

runners.name: Sawpit Samantha
runners.spotlight: 0-17 and she beat only one in 2m4f Warwick handicap chase in March; a change of headgear is adopted now.
runners.comment: 0-17 and she beat only one in 2m4f Warwick handicap chase in March

runners.name: Beep Beep Becky
runners.spotlight: Second in an Irish point but cut little ice in four runs over hurdles last season, beating only one on her handicap debut at Catterick (3m1f) in March; much more is required now going chasing with tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Cut little ice in four runs over hurdles last season; more required now going chasing

races.race_name: Carlsberg Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Beau Quali
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful winning chaser for Dan Skelton; in excellent form for his current yard too, finally getting off the mark over hurdles in 2m3f novice at Chepstow 22 days ago; the clear form pick.
runners.comment: In excellent form, off the mark over hurdles at Chepstow 22 days ago; the clear form pick

runners.name: Manalishi
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in sole bumper at Chepstow and has shaped with promise on both outings in this sphere, staying on well having stumbled home turn when second of six in 2m5f novice at Market Rasen last month; this son of Malinas has more to offer.
runners.comment: Promising second at Market Rasen last month; this son of Malinas has more to offer

runners.name: Chicker
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form when winning his first two bumpers but well held in Champion Bumper at Cheltenham and Grade 2 bumper at Aintree last two runs; has since had a wind op and remains with potential now going hurdling.
runners.comment: Fairly useful bumper winner; had a wind op and remains with potential now going hurdling

runners.name: Solo Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Spanish Moon gelding; promising debut bumper second at Huntingdon in February but he failed to build on it when sixth in similar event there following month; goes hurdling now.
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut bumper second when sixth at Huntingdon; goes hurdling now
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Double Tiger
runners.spotlight: Blue Bresil gelding who took a step forward when second of 15 in 2m Wetherby novice hurdle in March; capable of better still back up in trip so not out of things.
runners.comment: Very good second at Wetherby in March; capable of better still back up in trip

runners.name: Emerald Ocean
runners.spotlight: 12-1 and hooded, last of 12 in bumper here in March; this Blue Bresil mare needs to take a major step forward now going hurdling with a tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Last in bumper here in March; major step forward needed now going hurdling

runners.name: Executive Producer
runners.spotlight: Placed in an Irish point but little show in a pair of bumpers and on his hurdling debut in 2m4f Newcastle novice last month.
runners.comment: Little show in a pair of bumpers and on hurdling debut in 2m4f Newcastle novice last month

runners.name: The Hostage
runners.spotlight: Hinted at promise when a debut bumper sixth at Huntingdon in March but he came in last of 11 in similar company at Hereford 30 days ago; hurdling debutant.
runners.comment: Last of 11 in bumper at Hereford 30 days ago; hurdling debutant

runners.name: Final Surprise
runners.spotlight: Fair bumper performer who made a winning start in this sphere in 2m novice here two weeks ago; open to progress but more is needed under a 7lb penalty though.
runners.comment: Winning start in this sphere in novice here; more is needed under a 7lb penalty though

races.race_name: 1664 Biere Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Galway Reel
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough win over fences at Market Rasen (3m) in September and posted a good second in handicap chase at Leicester (2m7f) when last seen out in December; proved reliable over hurdles so rates a player back in this sphere off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Good chasing second at Leicester in December; reliable over hurdles so rates a player here

runners.name: Crack Ops
runners.spotlight: A fair bumper winner who has shown similar form when placed on all his three runs in this sphere, though he made mistakes when second of seven in 2m4f Bangor handicap four weeks ago; cheekpieces worn there are left off and he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Made mistakes when second at Bangor latest; cheekpieces left off and he can't be ruled out

runners.name: Country Mouse
runners.spotlight: Points scorer but she's failed to progress over hurdles and was tried in cheekpieces when pulled up in 2m5f Warwick handicap 23 days ago; headgear is quickly dispensed with.
runners.comment: Points scorer but she's failed to progress over hurdles; others appeal more here

runners.name: Barry Fife
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who wasn't discredited when third of seven in 2m novice here two weeks ago; goes handicapping now with this return to further a plus; not out of things.
runners.comment: Not discredited when third here latest; return to further is a plus so not out of things

runners.name: Getuptheyard
runners.spotlight: Maiden who fell in consecutive chases last autumn and was tailed off in 3m handicap hurdle on latest outing in November; has something to prove after a layoff; had wind surgery in March.
runners.comment: Fell in consecutive chases last autumn and was tailed off in 3m handicap hurdle last time

runners.name: Dexperado
runners.spotlight: 0-10 and has been off for 14 months since coming in a below-par sixth in 3m Southwell handicap hurdle; not easy to warm to.
runners.comment: Off 14 months since a below-par sixth in Southwell handicap hurdle; not easy to warm to

runners.name: Pepite De Saphir
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced mare who signed off for the Newland/Insole stable with a remote second of four in 2m4f handicap hurdle at Newcastle last February; needs to hit the ground running for her new yard; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Remote second at Newcastle last February; needs to hit the ground running for her new yard

races.race_name: Eaton Hall Shooting Ground Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Secret Trix
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner; only win last season was a walkover in a chase; went back hurdling after that and took time to find his form, but sound placed efforts on last two starts, latest when third at Haydock in March (3m, good; first run after a wind op) when Super Survivor was well behind in eighth; chance.
runners.comment: Creditable runs last twice, latest a third at Haydock on first run after wind op; chance

runners.name: Minella Rescue
runners.spotlight: Won this race off this mark in 2025 (good; 2-1 favourite) having run poorly on his previous start; was rather disappointing later on last season and has a bit to prove at present.
runners.comment: Won this off the same mark in 2025; rather disappointing later in the season; bit to prove

runners.name: Wa Wa
runners.spotlight: Veteran; mixes chasing with hurdling; sound run over hurdles when beaten a neck at Perth last time (3m2f, good); not out of it, but Sean Bowen rides stable companion Secret Trix.
runners.comment: Veteran; neck second over 3m2f at Perth last time; would have a chance on that form

runners.name: Lihyan
