runners.spotlight: Very progressive and unbeaten (4-4) in his first season last year, culminating in Group 2 win at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) where he won going away; finished third, tasting first defeat but not disgraced, at Sandown on reappearance; may resume improvement with that outing under his belt and show that he's still a likely candidate for high ranking in the miling division this term; big player.
runners.comment: May resume progress with Sandown reappearance under his belt; 4-4 previously; big player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Didn't have the ideal set-up at Sandown (behind Zeus Olympios) on reappearance but still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's Group 1 Queen Elizabeth II (1m, good) at Ascot, where he was possibly helped by the draw, can be backed up; may be flattered by his mark.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's QEII can be backed up

runners.name: The Lion In Winter
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in last year's Dante and Derby but subsequently showed very smart form back down in trip, including placed efforts in three top-level races over 1m, third to Notable Speech at the Breeders' Cup (firm) the final time; took advantage of a good opportunity in Leopardstown Listed event on reappearance; possibilities back up in class, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Placed in a few Group 1 races over 1m; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Jonquil
runners.spotlight: Mixed results in international races are flanked by wins in last two domestic attempts, namely Group 2 at Goodwood and Listed race at Ascot, both over 1m on good to firm; very useful but his figures seem to have settled at a ceiling that is below the required standard in this field.
runners.comment: Successful in last two domestic attempts but faces a stiffer assignment in this field

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Bright start to last season culminated in neck second when favourite for this contest; broadly not in the same form since, most recently last of six (behind two of today's opponents) when bidding for repeat success in Sandown Group 2; big revival is needed in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Close second in this race last year but needs a return to form in first-time headgear

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: No slouch but he has never raced above Listed level and looks like being a possible pacemaker for stablemate The Lion In Winter in this first Group attempt; clear bottom of this pack on handicap marks and should be swallowed up once his rivals get roaring.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on marks and is a possible pacemaker for The Lion In Winter
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Sahlan
runners.spotlight: French colt who has low mileage and could well show further improvement in this 4yo campaign; held on narrowly in the Group 1 Moulin at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) then ended 2025 season with creditable fifth to Notable Speech in the Breeders' Cup Mile (firm); interesting in this first British foray.
runners.comment: French colt who won the 2025 Moulin; could well show further improvement this term

races.race_name: Highclere Castle Gin Carnarvon Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only on good/firmer; progressive 2yo campaign featured 6f wins at Newbury (Listed) and Newmarket (Group 1 Middle Park Stakes); may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance (Group 3) under his belt; major player, despite conceding weight all round.
runners.comment: Middle Park winner; may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: Has rather compromised his chance by taking a good hold in 7f events this term, latest at Newbury (Group 3) when still running creditably in a hood that is now removed; skipped the Guineas option and heads down the Commonwealth Cup route instead; 2-2 previously, both wins over 6f in May 2025, including Curragh Group 3; retains potential.
runners.comment: Likely to benefit from this return to sprinting and retains potential; 2-2 over 6f

runners.name: Royal Fixation
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly over 6f on good to firm ground last year for Ed Walker, acquitting herself well at Group level after debut win; prevailed in the Lowther at York on penultimate start, then ran well in Group 1 Cheveley Park Stakes that was won by the subsequent 1,000 Guineas winner; respected at the weights on debut for new connections.
runners.comment: Progressive for Ed Walker; won the Lowther then ran well in the Cheveley Park Stakes

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Talented, albeit is a shade quirky, and made a mockery of his mark in Southwell handicap (6f, AW) on reappearance; eventually dropped away in Ascot Group 3 since but may still have more to offer; not written off.
runners.comment: Well ahead of his mark in AW handicap on reappearance; dropped away in Group 3 since

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Picked up a lot of prize-money during a busy 2yo season and ran respectably in 6f Group 3 at Ascot most recently; however, has probably already reached his ceiling, unlike some of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Has racked up a lot of prize-money but probably already reached his ceiling

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well on the bare figures at Newbury (Group 3) in latest 7f attempt and turf debut (raced on AW previously); however, needs to prove he can back up that form and cope with this new trip.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up latest 7f effort and cope with this new trip

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has performed creditably at Listed level since winning a notable maiden at York but needs improvement for the first-time headgear in this second start for new stable.
runners.comment: Irish colt who needs improvement for first-time headgear; second run for new yard
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Posted good performances (won/second) in sales races last term and ran creditably off top weight in Newmarket handicap (6f, good) on reappearance; however, has something to find on ratings in this field.
runners.comment: Form includes good performances in sales races; something to find on ratings
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Sirius A
runners.spotlight: Has a broadly solid record, latterly on AW; registered a neck success in useful conditions race at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) on seasonal debut; however, needs to show further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Won useful AW conditions race on reappearance; needs further progress back on turf

runners.name: Havana Hurricane
runners.spotlight: Held by Wise Approach on Group 1 Middle Park running (6f) but otherwise proved a useful and consistent sprinter last term, winning at Royal Ascot (Listed) and making the frame in several other notable races; solid claims, assuming he has trained on.
runners.comment: Useful and consistent sprinter last term; successful at Royal Ascot; solid claims

runners.name: Egoli
runners.spotlight: Gained one of his 2yo wins over C&D but had his limitations rather exposed in better races subsequently; gelded after final outing; stiff task on on reappearance, being bottom of this pack on ratings.
runners.comment: Limitations became exposed after C&D win; gelded since last run; bottom rated

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Yeats Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: The most experienced of these, having won Curragh 2yo maiden and twice campaigned in Group company, notably fifth in Group 1 Criterium International; underwhelming seasonal debut, however, in Epsom Classic Trial last month, weakening quite tamely final furlong; blinkers now tried and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Highest-rated but bit to prove after below-par seasonal return at Epsom
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Limestone
runners.spotlight: Progressed nicely in each of three juvenile runs, notably making all in Listowel rated race on heavy ground (1m); followed up on seasonal return when tongue-tied in 1m2f Cork conditions last month, dictating steady pace then battling well; Derby entry looks the type to keep progressing and could well improve for this longer trip.
runners.comment: Race to suit when winning at Cork; type to progress and could improve for this longer trip

runners.name: Asakir
runners.spotlight: Zarak gelding backed up a promising Curragh debut in March when made plenty of use of in beating a subsequent winner in 1m2f Leopardstown maiden last month, both those runs on softish ground; big step up in trip here and faces quicker conditions now.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden win franked; big step up in trip here and faces quicker ground
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ulysses colt didn't run at two but three runs already this term, showing plenty of improvement to land Ballinrobe maiden over this trip eight days ago, making virtually all to win handsomely (good-to-yielding); yard took same route with last year's winner Carmers en route to an Ascot success so demands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: Impressive Ballinrobe maiden winner; Carmers completed same double for yard last year

runners.name: Sangreal
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly a 270,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Roi De France (Listed-placed 1m inc AW; RPR 112) and Vaguely Royal (1m4f Flat inc AW, 100; 1m7f hurdle); dam 1m/8.5f winner (inc AW 2yo/US turf stakes; 97); promising debut run in Leopardstown fillies maiden last month when chasing home a clear winner over 1m2f; should improve and win races but this looks a big jump in grade.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden second should improve and win races but this looks a big jump in grade

runners.name: Star Of Tara
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in four maidens at two; completely out of her depth.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in four maidens at two; completely out of her depth

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Lockinge Stakes (Group 1)

runners.name: Notable Speech
runners.spotlight: High-class miler domestically and internationally, predominantly on good and quicker ground; lacked recent match practice when only fourth in this race 12 months ago; bagged the 2,000 Guineas and Sussex Stakes in 2024 and completed a North American Grade 1 double, including Breeders' Cup Mile success, last autumn; had no luck in running at Keeneland on reappearance; leading player on best figures.
runners.comment: High-class miler; successful four times at the top level; leading player on best figures

runners.name: More Thunder
runners.spotlight: Usually held up in rear; raced at 1m1f/1m2f for previous stable; record of 4-6 (over 6f/7f) last term for new yard featured wins in a major handicap at Newmarket and Group 2 at Newbury, both on good to firm ground; respectable fourth in the Group 1 Foret at Longchamp (7f, very soft) on final start; shapes as if this new trip is worth exploring and may rate higher still.
runners.comment: Did very well over 6f/7f last term for new stable; shapes as if 1m is worth exploring

runners.name: Damysus
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who comfortably defied a 3lb penalty in Group 3 event at Newmarket (good) on seasonal debut, completing a 1m2f/1m1f hat-trick and taking overall record to 4-7; gave the impression he'll be as effective returned to 1m; progressing well and his stable has landed this prize five times, including the last two years; commands respect.
runners.comment: Frankel colt who is progressing well; yard has a good record in this prize; respected

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: Very progressive and unbeaten (4-4) in his first season last year, culminating in Group 2 win at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) where he won going away; finished third, tasting first defeat but not disgraced, at Sandown on reappearance; may resume improvement with that outing under his belt and show that he's still a likely candidate for high ranking in the miling division this term; big player.
runners.comment: May resume progress with Sandown reappearance under his belt; 4-4 previously; big player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Didn't have the ideal set-up at Sandown (behind Zeus Olympios) on reappearance but still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's Group 1 Queen Elizabeth II (1m, good) at Ascot, where he was possibly helped by the draw, can be backed up; may be flattered by his mark.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's QEII can be backed up

runners.name: The Lion In Winter
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in last year's Dante and Derby but subsequently showed very smart form back down in trip, including placed efforts in three top-level races over 1m, third to Notable Speech at the Breeders' Cup (firm) the final time; took advantage of a good opportunity in Leopardstown Listed event on reappearance; possibilities back up in class, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Placed in a few Group 1 races over 1m; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Jonquil
runners.spotlight: Mixed results in international races are flanked by wins in last two domestic attempts, namely Group 2 at Goodwood and Listed race at Ascot, both over 1m on good to firm; very useful but his figures seem to have settled at a ceiling that is below the required standard in this field.
runners.comment: Successful in last two domestic attempts but faces a stiffer assignment in this field

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Bright start to last season culminated in neck second when favourite for this contest; broadly not in the same form since, most recently last of six (behind two of today's opponents) when bidding for repeat success in Sandown Group 2; big revival is needed in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Close second in this race last year but needs a return to form in first-time headgear

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: No slouch but he has never raced above Listed level and looks like being a possible pacemaker for stablemate The Lion In Winter in this first Group attempt; clear bottom of this pack on handicap marks and should be swallowed up once his rivals get roaring.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on marks and is a possible pacemaker for The Lion In Winter
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Sahlan
runners.spotlight: French colt who has low mileage and could well show further improvement in this 4yo campaign; held on narrowly in the Group 1 Moulin at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) then ended 2025 season with creditable fifth to Notable Speech in the Breeders' Cup Mile (firm); interesting in this first British foray.
runners.comment: French colt who won the 2025 Moulin; could well show further improvement this term

races.race_name: Read Meg Nicholls' Blog At betmgm.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Forty Years On
runners.spotlight: Two novice wins (7f/1m, good) before handicap debut at Ascot (7f, soft) completed her 2025 hat-trick; beaten a head at Southwell (7f, AW; fractious at stalls) final start; lightly raced 4yo who could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who has won three of her five starts and could have more to offer

runners.name: Golden Redemption
runners.spotlight: Placed on all six handicap starts, most recently second of six over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago when twice suffering interference from the winner in the closing stages; every chance he'll be bang there.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D recently when twice suffering interference from the winner late on

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: Won four in a row on AW this year before close fourth at Musselburgh (7f); merely midfield in a big field over this C&D 12 days later but he sat prominent in a race that favoured those ridden more patiently; not discounted.
runners.comment: In good form prior to C&D last time, when he had an excuse for his mid-division finish

runners.name: Arctic Dawn
runners.spotlight: Turf maiden winner who scored in fine style in a handicap at Newcastle (7f, AW) last October; on that evidence this 7lb higher mark isn't necessarily beyond him, but he needs to improve upon what he produced on his last two outings in November and December.
runners.comment: Won in fine style on AW last October but failed to back that up on next two starts

runners.name: I Still Have Faith
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but he's twice gone close on AW this year (7f/1m) and fared best of the eight-strong centre group when fifth of 13 here (1m, good to firm) a fortnight ago; might not be far away.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but some good form this season and he might not be far away
runners.owner: J Morley D Reynolds C Watkins & Partner

runners.name: Bobby Bennu
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent during light campaign last season but he's 3lb lower than when a close second at Haydock (7f, good) last July, so is handicapped to be in the mix if at the top of his game on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last season but handicapped to be in the mix if at the top of his game

runners.name: Son
runners.spotlight: 0-16 since winning in June 2023 on his second start but short-headed at Thirsk (7f, good) last month on his stable and seasonal debut and posted another good second at Haydock (7f, good) last Saturday; he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 0-16 since 2yo win but runner-up on both starts for this yard and he's on the shortlist

runners.name: Shah
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in 2024 on first three 2yo starts (7f/1m, good to firm/good to soft) before easily making all in 7f AW novice; off since, with gelding/wind ops in the meantime; it's a hefty absence to defy but he has potential off his opening mark if retaining his ability; the betting will be interesting.
runners.comment: Hefty absence to defy but he has potential off his opening mark if retaining his ability

runners.name: Silver Ghost
runners.spotlight: Two wins last May, including over C&D (good); failed to kick on subsequently but she reappeared with a solid third of six over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago and could build on that performance this afternoon.
runners.comment: C&D winner; reappeared with solid third over C&D and could build on that performance today

runners.name: Dark Tornado
runners.spotlight: Absent since below-par run last August on final start for Denis Quinn (same ownership); however, this lightly raced 5yo returns after wind surgery and he won this last year (good to firm); no surprise if he has a big say.
runners.comment: Absent since below-par run last August but won this last year; could go well on yard debut

runners.name: Angel Hunter
runners.spotlight: Has in-and-out profile and was only sixth at Ascot (1m, good to firm) a fortnight ago, but he's effective on turf and has won three of his last eight starts, most recently at Lingfield (1m, AW) last month; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Only 6th at Ascot a fortnight ago but three wins from last eight starts and not ruled out

runners.name: Superposition
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf and his best form (and both wins) has come on AW; below par on AW on last two starts and arrives with something to prove.
runners.comment: 0-7 on turf; below par on AW on last two starts and arrives with something to prove

runners.name: Darkness
runners.spotlight: Took a step back in the right direction with his close sixth at Haydock on latest start and he's back in the visor in which his last five wins have come, including two last season (7f, good to firm); entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Step back in the right direction last time and the return of the visor could be a positive

races.race_name: Highclere Castle Gin Carnarvon Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only on good/firmer; progressive 2yo campaign featured 6f wins at Newbury (Listed) and Newmarket (Group 1 Middle Park Stakes); may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance (Group 3) under his belt; major player, despite conceding weight all round.
runners.comment: Middle Park winner; may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: Has rather compromised his chance by taking a good hold in 7f events this term, latest at Newbury (Group 3) when still running creditably in a hood that is now removed; skipped the Guineas option and heads down the Commonwealth Cup route instead; 2-2 previously, both wins over 6f in May 2025, including Curragh Group 3; retains potential.
runners.comment: Likely to benefit from this return to sprinting and retains potential; 2-2 over 6f

runners.name: Royal Fixation
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly over 6f on good to firm ground last year for Ed Walker, acquitting herself well at Group level after debut win; prevailed in the Lowther at York on penultimate start, then ran well in Group 1 Cheveley Park Stakes that was won by the subsequent 1,000 Guineas winner; respected at the weights on debut for new connections.
runners.comment: Progressive for Ed Walker; won the Lowther then ran well in the Cheveley Park Stakes

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Talented, albeit is a shade quirky, and made a mockery of his mark in Southwell handicap (6f, AW) on reappearance; eventually dropped away in Ascot Group 3 since but may still have more to offer; not written off.
runners.comment: Well ahead of his mark in AW handicap on reappearance; dropped away in Group 3 since

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Picked up a lot of prize-money during a busy 2yo season and ran respectably in 6f Group 3 at Ascot most recently; however, has probably already reached his ceiling, unlike some of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Has racked up a lot of prize-money but probably already reached his ceiling

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well on the bare figures at Newbury (Group 3) in latest 7f attempt and turf debut (raced on AW previously); however, needs to prove he can back up that form and cope with this new trip.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up latest 7f effort and cope with this new trip

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has performed creditably at Listed level since winning a notable maiden at York but needs improvement for the first-time headgear in this second start for new stable.
runners.comment: Irish colt who needs improvement for first-time headgear; second run for new yard
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Posted good performances (won/second) in sales races last term and ran creditably off top weight in Newmarket handicap (6f, good) on reappearance; however, has something to find on ratings in this field.
runners.comment: Form includes good performances in sales races; something to find on ratings
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Sirius A
runners.spotlight: Has a broadly solid record, latterly on AW; registered a neck success in useful conditions race at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) on seasonal debut; however, needs to show further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Won useful AW conditions race on reappearance; needs further progress back on turf

runners.name: Havana Hurricane
runners.spotlight: Held by Wise Approach on Group 1 Middle Park running (6f) but otherwise proved a useful and consistent sprinter last term, winning at Royal Ascot (Listed) and making the frame in several other notable races; solid claims, assuming he has trained on.
runners.comment: Useful and consistent sprinter last term; successful at Royal Ascot; solid claims

runners.name: Egoli
runners.spotlight: Gained one of his 2yo wins over C&D but had his limitations rather exposed in better races subsequently; gelded after final outing; stiff task on on reappearance, being bottom of this pack on ratings.
runners.comment: Limitations became exposed after C&D win; gelded since last run; bottom rated

races.race_name: Download The BetMGM App Handicap

runners.name: Arantes Nascimento
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three times in a row over 7f on AW in the winter and returned to form with a win at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; unproven over 6f and 0-10 on turf, but placed on grass a few times and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Won over 7f on AW last month; unproven over 6f but he's not ruled out

runners.name: Rajaking
runners.spotlight: Below par on AW last autumn on final two runs for Stuart Williams; however, he twice went close on the other Newmarket course (6f, good to firm) last August and he'll be bang there if rediscovering that form on his stable and seasonal debut, for which he wears a tongue-tie; remains in same ownership.
runners.comment: Below par last autumn but bang there if rediscovering August form on this stable debut

runners.name: Pressure's On
runners.spotlight: Three-time 7f winner who was runner-up twice in a row over 7f on AW in March; however, below best over 1m/7f on his last two starts and this is a rare crack at a sprint trip; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Three-time 7f winner; below best over 1m/7f on last two starts; rare crack at sprint trip

runners.name: Tabby
runners.spotlight: All seven starts on AW; went close on stable debut at Wolverhampton (6f) last month after a long absence then won at Southwell (6f); this lightly raced 4yo could have more to offer for her current yard; respected on turf debut.
runners.comment: Won on AW on second start back from long absence; lightly raced & could have more to offer

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Three 6f wins towards the end of last year (including on good to soft/good to firm); took a step back in the right direction when third of seven at Southwell (7f, AW) last time and may be able to build on that run today; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Three wins towards the end of last year; step back in right direction latest; e-w possible
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Izzy Fast
runners.spotlight: 4yo who made debut last October and improved upon her first two runs when dropped in trip at Yarmouth (6f, good) last month, when she won by a neck at 50-1; had a next-time-out winner in second last time and this unexposed filly could be involved on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Won 6f novice at Yarmouth when dropped in trip; unexposed and could be involved

runners.name: Dashing Dick
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way at Leicester three weeks ago on his first run of the season but heavy reappearance defeats have been a recurring theme in his profile; won over C&D (good to soft) on final outing last year and he's also run well here on fast ground; could bounce back with a big run.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on final outing last term and it's very likely he needed his reappearance run

runners.name: Expert Agent
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (6f, AW) last month and second of 11 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) next time; this in-form 6yo is one to consider.
runners.comment: Won on AW last month and second at Windsor since; this in-form 6yo is one to consider
runners.owner: J P Morris, Mrs V A Tunnicliffe Et Al

runners.name: Sixtygeesbaby
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D (good) last September on final run for Tom Clover and now 3lb lower; David O'Meara should find the key to him at some point but he's been below his best on his first three runs for the yard.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D last September off 3lb higher but below best so far this season

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: Ran well when fifth at Chester (6f, good) last Friday, when denied a clear run on his second stable start; Chester has brought out the best him in the past but he can also be effective elsewhere and could be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Unlucky fifth at Chester on second stable start and he could be thereabouts

runners.name: Rebel Path
runners.spotlight: Tame end to last season and no return to form on his two starts this year; however, he's 2lb lower than when winning this under Olivia Haines last year (good to firm) and the return to the Rowley Mile could trigger a revival.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but won this last year and return to this C&D could trigger revival

races.race_name: Trade Nation London Gold Cup Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Azd
runners.spotlight: Looks quirky but he followed three seconds at 1m/1m2f by asserting late on in a 1m3f maiden at Southwell (AW) in April and he stormed clear in seven-runner handicap at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) two weeks later; blinkered on last three starts; up 7lb and drops back in trip, which has to be a worry, but he's certainly got his act together.
runners.comment: Up 7lb and back in trip (a concern) but he's got his act together and is on a hat-trick

runners.name: Sahara King
runners.spotlight: AW winner in November who finished only just behind My Love Is King in blanket finish to a hot novice over C&D (good) on reappearance; again came from the rear when second of five in handicap at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) 13 days ago, which was creditable but he needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Only just behind My Love Is King over C&D; creditable 2nd of five in handicap at Newmarket
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Lost Boys
runners.spotlight: Plenty of ability all 2yo starts, on final occasion always front rank to win a maiden at Haydock (1m, good to soft); dam was a 1m2f/1m4f winner and he duly improved at 1m2f when reappearing in a six-runner handicap at Sandown (good) three weeks ago, leading over 1f out and just holding on; up 6lb but should have more improvement in him.
runners.comment: Up 6lb since his narrow 1m2f win at Sandown but should have more improvement in him

runners.name: Sintra
runners.spotlight: Made debut in February; advanced his RPR on all three starts in maiden/novice events and made a winning handicap debut at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last Saturday; that was just a five-runner race and he is 9lb higher today, including 4lb out of the handicap, but the win came in pretty taking style.
runners.comment: 9lb higher today, including 4lb out of handicap, but Ascot win came in pretty taking style

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: All six races on good or softer; 1m2f suited him well on final two runs as 2yo, culminating in a Nottingham handicap win; not discredited when out of his depth in Listed race at Epsom (1m2f, good) on reappearance but he needs to resume marked improvement from 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Not discredited in Listed race on return; 2lb wrong; needs to resume marked improvement

runners.name: Bourbon Blues
runners.spotlight: Basically progressive as 2yo, ending with best form over 1m2f/1m1f, and he reappeared with a creditable third of seven in 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket one month ago; after ten races, though, he may well be more exposed than a lot of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Progressive as 2yo and ran creditably on reappearance but he should be relatively exposed
runners.owner: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Yarmouth maiden winner who was fourth in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) on his third 2yo start; put firmly in his place in Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) on reappearance but was 20-1 that day, may have needed the run and could be of more interest on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: May have needed Gr3 reappearance run and could be of more interest on this handicap debut

runners.name: Capall Rasa
runners.spotlight: Hope for 1m2f on pedigree; ended last term with 7f and 1m AW wins; led over 2f out to over 1f out when creditable fourth of seven in handicap at Sandown (1m, good; 25-1) three weeks ago, and perhaps he needed that race, but others seem to have more striking claims.
runners.comment: Led over 2f out to over 1f out when creditable fourth of seven at Sandown (1m, good; 25-1)

runners.name: Port Of Spain
runners.spotlight: Won a Punchestown maiden (1m, good to yielding) in September on third start; came up short in his three subsequent races but they were all Group events (1m/1m1f) and he improved in terms of ratings; cheekpieces go on today; although his Derby entry may not mean a great deal, as he's one of 16 from his top stable, they won this race in 2023 and 1m2f+ can suit.
runners.comment: Came up short on last three 2yo races but they were all Group events; 1m2f+ can suit
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Tierra Del Toro
runners.spotlight: Cost 340,000gns as a yearling, with dam a sister to dual Arc winner Treve; progressive in his three 2yo races, winning a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) before his rallying second in a notable conditions stakes here (1m, good to soft; just ahead of Bourbon Blues); gelded later; makes his reappearance but 1m2f+ should be a big help, so he's one to note.
runners.comment: Gelded; makes his reappearance but 1m2f+ should be a big help, so he's one to note

races.race_name: Hong Kong Jockey Club World Pool Handicap

runners.name: Far Above Dream
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism (6f, good) on seasonal debut, taking Goodwood record to 3-3 and overall strike-rate to 4-12; a possible danger provided his progress can be sustained away from that track.
runners.comment: Reappearance win took Goodwood record to 3-3; a  danger if progress sustained elsewhere

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame at Newcastle (AW; behind Wiltshire) and Leicester since being gelded; good fourth in the Listed Carnarvon Stakes on this card last year; 0-3 on turf but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in two starts since being gelded; each-way claims
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who is a three-time winner; gelded prior to second-place finishes over 6f at Newbury (beaten a head by Mirabeau) and Newmarket (behind a handicap snip) this term; solid claims.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to second place finishes at Newbury and Newmarket this term; solid claims

runners.name: Realign
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who was gelded prior to Haydock win in penultimate 2025 start; may resume progress with Newmarket reappearance under his belt; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who may resume progress with Newmarket reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Successful in major handicap in 2023 and a few Group races since but balance of 2026 form suggests he's unlikely to defy top weight in this field.
runners.comment: Balance of 2026 form suggests he's unlikely to defy top weight in this field

runners.name: Dark Ace
runners.spotlight: Irish handicapper whose AW double in Britain this year has taken his record since being gelded to 3-6; improving and can't be dismissed, despite being a stone higher than for last turf attempt (which he won).
runners.comment: Irish handicapper; AW double this year has taken his record since being gelded to 3-6
runners.owner: J Fairhurst & Damian McCann & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day but is on a long losing run and has a doubt over current form; by no means sure to take advantage of a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Useful on his day but is on a long losing run and has a doubt over current form
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Wiltshire
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery prior to 6f success on finals day at Newcastle last time, taking AW record to 4-9; no certainty to follow up, being only 1-8 on turf and back on a tougher mark.
runners.comment: Win on finals day took his AW record to 4-9; only 1-8 on turf and back on stiffer mark

runners.name: Mirabeau
runners.spotlight: Narrowly regained the winning thread in big field over C&D (good) last time when dropped back to 6f for first time since debut; unexposed at this trip and still looks favourably treated back up just 3lb; respected.
runners.comment: Scored narrowly over C&D last time; unexposed at this trip and still favourably treated
runners.owner: The Ffrench Revolution & Mrs T Hamilton

runners.name: Purosangue
runners.spotlight: Made late headway from the rear over 5f at Pontefract on stable/seasonal debut; interesting back up in trip, despite still being on a long losing spell; remains attractively treated on plenty of form.
runners.comment: Made late headway over 5f on stable/seasonal debut; interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Albasheer
runners.spotlight: Has moved in the right direction on AW since returning from lengthy layoff but the form suggests he doesn't retain peak ability; the 8yo is the oldest contender back on turf.
runners.comment: Probably doesn't retain peak ability; the oldest contender back on turf

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: Proved somewhat resurgent at Ascot last week in third start for Charlie Pike and may build on that effort; only 1lb above last winning mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Proved somewhat resurgent last week in third start for Charlie Pike; may build on that

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in big-field Class 5 event at Thirsk (6f, good) last month on second start for new yard; back up sharply in grade but gets weight all round and may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Thirsk last month on second start for new yard; may remain competitive

runners.name: Thunder Blue
runners.spotlight: Capable of fairly useful form but finished last of 18 over C&D in final start for Jamie Osborne; sold for 18,000gns later in October; headgear switch; best watched.
runners.comment: Finished last of 18 over C&D in final start for Jamie Osborne; debut for new yard

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably at Windsor five days ago but still has a modest strike-rate for current yard and hasn't won on British soil for two years.
runners.comment: Ran respectably five days ago but still has a modest strike-rate for current yard

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Aston Park Stakes (Group 3)

runners.name: West Wind Blows
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; dual Group 3 winner (2022 and 2023) who missed 2024 and most of 2025; regular action since November, including a Listed win and matching career-best form when second to mighty Calandagan at Meydan (1m4f Group 1, good); not so hot on softer ground at Longchamp (evens) 13 days ago but will give them all plenty to think about if straight back to his best.
runners.comment: Not so hot 13 days ago; will give them plenty to think about if straight back to his best

runners.name: Kalpana
runners.spotlight: Had excellent campaigns in 2024 and 2025, both of which ended with Group 1 success in good style against her own sex on Champions Day at Ascot; she also started last season with a big run in an Irish Group 1 and was an excellent second in the King George at Ascot in July; wore cheekpieces last two starts; concedes 4lb to male rivals today but probably still the one to beat.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 on another high note; gives 4lb to male rivals; probably still the one to beat

runners.name: Arabian Force
runners.spotlight: Progressed very well as 3yo last term, culminating in his close third in Group 2 Great Voltigeur at York (1m4f, good to firm) in August, until not so good trying 1m6f in Listed race on final outing; always close up and rallied when close second in 1m4f Listed event at Longchamp on reappearance; may well have more to give but it is needed in today's company.
runners.comment: Progressive last term; may well have more to give but it is needed in today's company

runners.name: French Master
runners.spotlight: Won two 1m6f handicaps last term, latterly in taking style from off the pace at Royal Ascot (good to firm) when wearing headgear first time; gelded and back to that sort of form when third of six in Group 2 at Newmarket (1m4f) 15 days ago, albeit not getting to grips with the leaders; no headgear that day (he has also tried a visor) but he's now fitted with first-time cheekpieces; bit to find if others are on their game.
runners.comment: Tries a third type of headgear, after a creditable reappearance without; bit to find

runners.name: Liberty Lane
runners.spotlight: Usually races prominently; a mostly solid 2025 included 1m2f Listed wins in May and November; 2026 started in downbeat fashion (similar to last year) in Listed race and his top three ratings have been registered on softer than good; this is also a new trip (unraced beyond an extended 1m2f) but he's bred to get it and has a chance on best form; ground the chief worry.
runners.comment: Bred to get this new trip but top three ratings have been registered on softer than good
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Childwickbury Stud Fillies' Trial Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Esna
runners.spotlight: All three starts as 2yo were on soft, winning a Sandown novice but far better form (the best in this field) when about 2l fourth of eight in 1m Group 1 Marcel Boussac at Longchamp, keeping on well while never able to threaten seriously; fair chance she'll stay 1m2f, judged on that, but she didn't show it when a never-dangerous fourth of six in Newmarket Listed race (good to firm; just over 1l behind Sacred Ground) 13 days ago; hopefully she'll have come on significantly from that run.
runners.comment: 1m 2yo Group 1 fourth at Longchamp, best form in this; just behind Sacred Ground on return

runners.name: Sacred Ground
runners.spotlight: By Kingman out of Oaks winner Anapurna; favourite for all three starts and won on debut; second since in Newmarket Listed races over 1m (good to soft) in November and 1m2f (good to firm) 13 days ago, in the latter behind a runaway winner in six-runner field with Esna just over a length back in fourth; better form claims than most and still some potential.
runners.comment: 2nd in two Newmarket Listed races; better form claims than most and still some potential

runners.name: Allaire
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners including Eldar Eldarov (1m-14.5f including St Leger; RPR 117) and Kingswood (1m1f/1m2f; 118); second from towards rear in a novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and the same when making most in C&D maiden (good) four weeks ago; no match for serious talents in those races; today's rivals might not look quite as good but she needs to improve markedly.
runners.comment: 2nd twice; today's rivals might not look quite as good as those winners; needs to improve

runners.name: Fractional
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree and fair form when never dangerous at Doncaster (7f, good) in September on debut; not so good when front-running at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) six weeks later, hanging markedly left;  likely to stay but she needs to leave both pieces of form well behind her.
runners.comment: Needs to leave both pieces of 2yo form (better on debut) well behind her; likely to stay

runners.name: Lady Roisia
runners.spotlight: 575,000gns foal who's a half-sister to four winners, notably Ambition (1m2f-1m4f, including French Group 2), out of the yard's Oaks winner Talent; followed debut promise by justifying 5-6 favouritism in seven-runner maiden at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in October; this grade should be a very different proposition for her but it's the same for most of this field.
runners.comment: Dam won the Oaks; 5-6, won maiden at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) on second 2yo start

runners.name: Golden Orbit
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars half-sister to four winners, three useful; dam 7f-1m2f scorer (including Listed); 11-2 for eight-runner maiden at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last September and challenged 2f out before holding off a pretty useful rival; that was a fine start, including as she's bred for at least 1m2f.
runners.comment: Won a Newmarket maiden (7f, good to firm) last September and she's bred for at least 1m2f
runners.owner: HM The King & HM The Queen

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Lockinge Stakes (Group 1)

runners.name: Notable Speech
runners.spotlight: High-class miler domestically and internationally, predominantly on good and quicker ground; lacked recent match practice when only fourth in this race 12 months ago; bagged the 2,000 Guineas and Sussex Stakes in 2024 and completed a North American Grade 1 double, including Breeders' Cup Mile success, last autumn; had no luck in running at Keeneland on reappearance; leading player on best figures.
runners.comment: High-class miler; successful four times at the top level; leading player on best figures

runners.name: More Thunder
runners.spotlight: Usually held up in rear; raced at 1m1f/1m2f for previous stable; record of 4-6 (over 6f/7f) last term for new yard featured wins in a major handicap at Newmarket and Group 2 at Newbury, both on good to firm ground; respectable fourth in the Group 1 Foret at Longchamp (7f, very soft) on final start; shapes as if this new trip is worth exploring and may rate higher still.
runners.comment: Did very well over 6f/7f last term for new stable; shapes as if 1m is worth exploring

runners.name: Damysus
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt who comfortably defied a 3lb penalty in Group 3 event at Newmarket (good) on seasonal debut, completing a 1m2f/1m1f hat-trick and taking overall record to 4-7; gave the impression he'll be as effective returned to 1m; progressing well and his stable has landed this prize five times, including the last two years; commands respect.
runners.comment: Frankel colt who is progressing well; yard has a good record in this prize; respected

runners.name: Zeus Olympios
runners.spotlight: Very progressive and unbeaten (4-4) in his first season last year, culminating in Group 2 win at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) where he won going away; finished third, tasting first defeat but not disgraced, at Sandown on reappearance; may resume improvement with that outing under his belt and show that he's still a likely candidate for high ranking in the miling division this term; big player.
runners.comment: May resume progress with Sandown reappearance under his belt; 4-4 previously; big player
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Cicero's Gift
runners.spotlight: Didn't have the ideal set-up at Sandown (behind Zeus Olympios) on reappearance but still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's Group 1 Queen Elizabeth II (1m, good) at Ascot, where he was possibly helped by the draw, can be backed up; may be flattered by his mark.
runners.comment: Still needs to prove the form of his 100-1 success in last year's QEII can be backed up

runners.name: The Lion In Winter
runners.spotlight: Disappointing in last year's Dante and Derby but subsequently showed very smart form back down in trip, including placed efforts in three top-level races over 1m, third to Notable Speech at the Breeders' Cup (firm) the final time; took advantage of a good opportunity in Leopardstown Listed event on reappearance; possibilities back up in class, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Placed in a few Group 1 races over 1m; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Jonquil
runners.spotlight: Mixed results in international races are flanked by wins in last two domestic attempts, namely Group 2 at Goodwood and Listed race at Ascot, both over 1m on good to firm; very useful but his figures seem to have settled at a ceiling that is below the required standard in this field.
runners.comment: Successful in last two domestic attempts but faces a stiffer assignment in this field

runners.name: Dancing Gemini
runners.spotlight: Bright start to last season culminated in neck second when favourite for this contest; broadly not in the same form since, most recently last of six (behind two of today's opponents) when bidding for repeat success in Sandown Group 2; big revival is needed in first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Close second in this race last year but needs a return to form in first-time headgear

runners.name: Mississippi River
runners.spotlight: No slouch but he has never raced above Listed level and looks like being a possible pacemaker for stablemate The Lion In Winter in this first Group attempt; clear bottom of this pack on handicap marks and should be swallowed up once his rivals get roaring.
runners.comment: Bottom of this pack on marks and is a possible pacemaker for The Lion In Winter
runners.owner: D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & M Tabor & Peter M Brant

runners.name: Sahlan
runners.spotlight: French colt who has low mileage and could well show further improvement in this 4yo campaign; held on narrowly in the Group 1 Moulin at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) then ended 2025 season with creditable fifth to Notable Speech in the Breeders' Cup Mile (firm); interesting in this first British foray.
runners.comment: French colt who won the 2025 Moulin; could well show further improvement this term

races.race_name: Highclere Castle Gin Carnarvon Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Wise Approach
runners.spotlight: Campaigned only on good/firmer; progressive 2yo campaign featured 6f wins at Newbury (Listed) and Newmarket (Group 1 Middle Park Stakes); may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance (Group 3) under his belt; major player, despite conceding weight all round.
runners.comment: Middle Park winner; may be back in top form with Ascot reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Albert Einstein
runners.spotlight: Has rather compromised his chance by taking a good hold in 7f events this term, latest at Newbury (Group 3) when still running creditably in a hood that is now removed; skipped the Guineas option and heads down the Commonwealth Cup route instead; 2-2 previously, both wins over 6f in May 2025, including Curragh Group 3; retains potential.
runners.comment: Likely to benefit from this return to sprinting and retains potential; 2-2 over 6f

runners.name: Royal Fixation
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly over 6f on good to firm ground last year for Ed Walker, acquitting herself well at Group level after debut win; prevailed in the Lowther at York on penultimate start, then ran well in Group 1 Cheveley Park Stakes that was won by the subsequent 1,000 Guineas winner; respected at the weights on debut for new connections.
runners.comment: Progressive for Ed Walker; won the Lowther then ran well in the Cheveley Park Stakes

runners.name: Ghost Mode
runners.spotlight: Talented, albeit is a shade quirky, and made a mockery of his mark in Southwell handicap (6f, AW) on reappearance; eventually dropped away in Ascot Group 3 since but may still have more to offer; not written off.
runners.comment: Well ahead of his mark in AW handicap on reappearance; dropped away in Group 3 since

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Picked up a lot of prize-money during a busy 2yo season and ran respectably in 6f Group 3 at Ascot most recently; however, has probably already reached his ceiling, unlike some of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Has racked up a lot of prize-money but probably already reached his ceiling

runners.name: Aqpan
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well on the bare figures at Newbury (Group 3) in latest 7f attempt and turf debut (raced on AW previously); however, needs to prove he can back up that form and cope with this new trip.
runners.comment: Needs to prove he can back up latest 7f effort and cope with this new trip

runners.name: Frescobaldi
runners.spotlight: Irish colt who has performed creditably at Listed level since winning a notable maiden at York but needs improvement for the first-time headgear in this second start for new stable.
runners.comment: Irish colt who needs improvement for first-time headgear; second run for new yard
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell & R P Behan & Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Song Of The Clyde
runners.spotlight: Posted good performances (won/second) in sales races last term and ran creditably off top weight in Newmarket handicap (6f, good) on reappearance; however, has something to find on ratings in this field.
runners.comment: Form includes good performances in sales races; something to find on ratings
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Cxx & M Parker

runners.name: Sirius A
runners.spotlight: Has a broadly solid record, latterly on AW; registered a neck success in useful conditions race at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) on seasonal debut; however, needs to show further improvement back on turf.
runners.comment: Won useful AW conditions race on reappearance; needs further progress back on turf

runners.name: Havana Hurricane
runners.spotlight: Held by Wise Approach on Group 1 Middle Park running (6f) but otherwise proved a useful and consistent sprinter last term, winning at Royal Ascot (Listed) and making the frame in several other notable races; solid claims, assuming he has trained on.
runners.comment: Useful and consistent sprinter last term; successful at Royal Ascot; solid claims

runners.name: Egoli
runners.spotlight: Gained one of his 2yo wins over C&D but had his limitations rather exposed in better races subsequently; gelded after final outing; stiff task on on reappearance, being bottom of this pack on ratings.
runners.comment: Limitations became exposed after C&D win; gelded since last run; bottom rated

races.race_name: Trade Nation London Gold Cup Handicap (Heritage Handicap) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Al Azd
runners.spotlight: Looks quirky but he followed three seconds at 1m/1m2f by asserting late on in a 1m3f maiden at Southwell (AW) in April and he stormed clear in seven-runner handicap at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) two weeks later; blinkered on last three starts; up 7lb and drops back in trip, which has to be a worry, but he's certainly got his act together.
runners.comment: Up 7lb and back in trip (a concern) but he's got his act together and is on a hat-trick

runners.name: Sahara King
runners.spotlight: AW winner in November who finished only just behind My Love Is King in blanket finish to a hot novice over C&D (good) on reappearance; again came from the rear when second of five in handicap at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) 13 days ago, which was creditable but he needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Only just behind My Love Is King over C&D; creditable 2nd of five in handicap at Newmarket
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Lost Boys
runners.spotlight: Plenty of ability all 2yo starts, on final occasion always front rank to win a maiden at Haydock (1m, good to soft); dam was a 1m2f/1m4f winner and he duly improved at 1m2f when reappearing in a six-runner handicap at Sandown (good) three weeks ago, leading over 1f out and just holding on; up 6lb but should have more improvement in him.
runners.comment: Up 6lb since his narrow 1m2f win at Sandown but should have more improvement in him

runners.name: Sintra
runners.spotlight: Made debut in February; advanced his RPR on all three starts in maiden/novice events and made a winning handicap debut at Ascot (1m2f, good to firm) last Saturday; that was just a five-runner race and he is 9lb higher today, including 4lb out of the handicap, but the win came in pretty taking style.
runners.comment: 9lb higher today, including 4lb out of handicap, but Ascot win came in pretty taking style

runners.name: Pearl River
runners.spotlight: All six races on good or softer; 1m2f suited him well on final two runs as 2yo, culminating in a Nottingham handicap win; not discredited when out of his depth in Listed race at Epsom (1m2f, good) on reappearance but he needs to resume marked improvement from 2lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Not discredited in Listed race on return; 2lb wrong; needs to resume marked improvement

runners.name: Bourbon Blues
runners.spotlight: Basically progressive as 2yo, ending with best form over 1m2f/1m1f, and he reappeared with a creditable third of seven in 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket one month ago; after ten races, though, he may well be more exposed than a lot of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Progressive as 2yo and ran creditably on reappearance but he should be relatively exposed
runners.owner: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Yarmouth maiden winner who was fourth in a Group 3 at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) on his third 2yo start; put firmly in his place in Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) on reappearance but was 20-1 that day, may have needed the run and could be of more interest on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: May have needed Gr3 reappearance run and could be of more interest on this handicap debut

runners.name: Capall Rasa
runners.spotlight: Hope for 1m2f on pedigree; ended last term with 7f and 1m AW wins; led over 2f out to over 1f out when creditable fourth of seven in handicap at Sandown (1m, good; 25-1) three weeks ago, and perhaps he needed that race, but others seem to have more striking claims.
runners.comment: Led over 2f out to over 1f out when creditable fourth of seven at Sandown (1m, good; 25-1)

runners.name: Port Of Spain
runners.spotlight: Won a Punchestown maiden (1m, good to yielding) in September on third start; came up short in his three subsequent races but they were all Group events (1m/1m1f) and he improved in terms of ratings; cheekpieces go on today; although his Derby entry may not mean a great deal, as he's one of 16 from his top stable, they won this race in 2023 and 1m2f+ can suit.
runners.comment: Came up short on last three 2yo races but they were all Group events; 1m2f+ can suit
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Tierra Del Toro
runners.spotlight: Cost 340,000gns as a yearling, with dam a sister to dual Arc winner Treve; progressive in his three 2yo races, winning a novice at Kempton (7f, AW) before his rallying second in a notable conditions stakes here (1m, good to soft; just ahead of Bourbon Blues); gelded later; makes his reappearance but 1m2f+ should be a big help, so he's one to note.
runners.comment: Gelded; makes his reappearance but 1m2f+ should be a big help, so he's one to note

races.race_name: Hong Kong Jockey Club World Pool Handicap

runners.name: Far Above Dream
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism (6f, good) on seasonal debut, taking Goodwood record to 3-3 and overall strike-rate to 4-12; a possible danger provided his progress can be sustained away from that track.
runners.comment: Reappearance win took Goodwood record to 3-3; a  danger if progress sustained elsewhere

runners.name: Berkshire Whisper
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame at Newcastle (AW; behind Wiltshire) and Leicester since being gelded; good fourth in the Listed Carnarvon Stakes on this card last year; 0-3 on turf but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in two starts since being gelded; each-way claims
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Addison Grey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who is a three-time winner; gelded prior to second-place finishes over 6f at Newbury (beaten a head by Mirabeau) and Newmarket (behind a handicap snip) this term; solid claims.
runners.comment: Gelded prior to second place finishes at Newbury and Newmarket this term; solid claims

runners.name: Realign
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who was gelded prior to Haydock win in penultimate 2025 start; may resume progress with Newmarket reappearance under his belt; possibilities if taking well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who may resume progress with Newmarket reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Annaf
runners.spotlight: Successful in major handicap in 2023 and a few Group races since but balance of 2026 form suggests he's unlikely to defy top weight in this field.
runners.comment: Balance of 2026 form suggests he's unlikely to defy top weight in this field

runners.name: Dark Ace
runners.spotlight: Irish handicapper whose AW double in Britain this year has taken his record since being gelded to 3-6; improving and can't be dismissed, despite being a stone higher than for last turf attempt (which he won).
runners.comment: Irish handicapper; AW double this year has taken his record since being gelded to 3-6
runners.owner: J Fairhurst & Damian McCann & Cyril McMullan

runners.name: Korker
runners.spotlight: Useful on his day but is on a long losing run and has a doubt over current form; by no means sure to take advantage of a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Useful on his day but is on a long losing run and has a doubt over current form
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Wiltshire
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery prior to 6f success on finals day at Newcastle last time, taking AW record to 4-9; no certainty to follow up, being only 1-8 on turf and back on a tougher mark.
runners.comment: Win on finals day took his AW record to 4-9; only 1-8 on turf and back on stiffer mark

runners.name: Mirabeau
runners.spotlight: Narrowly regained the winning thread in big field over C&D (good) last time when dropped back to 6f for first time since debut; unexposed at this trip and still looks favourably treated back up just 3lb; respected.
runners.comment: Scored narrowly over C&D last time; unexposed at this trip and still favourably treated
runners.owner: The Ffrench Revolution & Mrs T Hamilton

runners.name: Purosangue
runners.spotlight: Made late headway from the rear over 5f at Pontefract on stable/seasonal debut; interesting back up in trip, despite still being on a long losing spell; remains attractively treated on plenty of form.
runners.comment: Made late headway over 5f on stable/seasonal debut; interesting back up in trip

runners.name: Albasheer
runners.spotlight: Has moved in the right direction on AW since returning from lengthy layoff but the form suggests he doesn't retain peak ability; the 8yo is the oldest contender back on turf.
runners.comment: Probably doesn't retain peak ability; the oldest contender back on turf

runners.name: Brosay
runners.spotlight: Proved somewhat resurgent at Ascot last week in third start for Charlie Pike and may build on that effort; only 1lb above last winning mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Proved somewhat resurgent last week in third start for Charlie Pike; may build on that

runners.name: Yes I'm Mali
runners.spotlight: Prevailed in big-field Class 5 event at Thirsk (6f, good) last month on second start for new yard; back up sharply in grade but gets weight all round and may remain competitive.
runners.comment: Prevailed at Thirsk last month on second start for new yard; may remain competitive

runners.name: Thunder Blue
runners.spotlight: Capable of fairly useful form but finished last of 18 over C&D in final start for Jamie Osborne; sold for 18,000gns later in October; headgear switch; best watched.
runners.comment: Finished last of 18 over C&D in final start for Jamie Osborne; debut for new yard

runners.name: Desert Cop
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably at Windsor five days ago but still has a modest strike-rate for current yard and hasn't won on British soil for two years.
runners.comment: Ran respectably five days ago but still has a modest strike-rate for current yard

races.race_name: Boyle Sports Home Of The Early Payout Handicap

runners.name: Spanish Voice
runners.spotlight: Six races overall and his only two on turf resulted in one front-running win at Newmarket last April (1m2f, good) and another two weeks ago (1m, good to firm); being dropped back in trip worked a treat last time and, although hit with a 9lb rise, better still may well be forthcoming.
runners.comment: Dropping back to 1m worked a treat when he won well at Newmarket two weeks ago; up 9lb

runners.name: Blue Rc
runners.spotlight: Has improved his rating almost every time, on last two outings taking second in strong 1m handicaps at Newcastle (AW) and Thirsk (good to firm); came from almost last when closing to half a length in 13-runner race last time and looks well capable of a prominent finish here despite 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Second in strong 1m handicaps at Newcastle (AW) and Thirsk (good to firm); keeps improving

runners.name: Bullet Point
runners.spotlight: Can front-run; had 1m wins at Newmarket and York last season, with two seconds at Ascot in between including the Royal Hunt Cup; makes plenty of appeal on that evidence but needs to bounce back from an underwhelming reappearance when second favourite at Newmarket two weeks ago.
runners.comment: Plenty of appeal on 2025 evidence; needs to bounce back from an underwhelming reappearance

runners.name: Mezcala
runners.spotlight: Outstayed over 1m1f on final start last term, which was also his only race on good to firm; progressive at 1m otherwise and laughed at his 21 rivals in the Spring Mile at Doncaster (good to soft) on reappearance seven weeks ago; up 8lb but that win came with huge authority and he's still low mileage.
runners.comment: Laughed at 21 rivals in Spring Mile at Doncaster (good to soft) seven weeks ago; up 8lb

runners.name: Classic
runners.spotlight: Third in last year's Spring Cup over C&D and fourth in this race; next three starts were good as well and he was better than ever for reappearance second of 24 in the Spring Cup four weeks ago; up just 1lb, a leading player.
runners.comment: Better than ever for second of 24 in the Spring Cup four weeks ago; up just 1lb

runners.name: Man Of La Mancha
runners.spotlight: Won three of his seven handicaps (1m-1m1f) last term, including the last two; just fourth of 13 to Spanish Voice when favourite at Newmarket on reappearance but that was respectable; player on peak 2025 effort.
runners.comment: Did well last term; fourth of 13 to Spanish Voice when favourite at Newmarket on return

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: 1-18 on turf but his only two appearances at this track saw him take third in the Spring Cup and this race in 2024; operating a bit below that level since last August, which, despite some AW success, has to put him down the pecking order in such a hot race.
runners.comment: Did well here spring 2024 but 1-18 on turf; down the pecking order despite some AW success

runners.name: Indalo
runners.spotlight: Progressive most starts in 2025 and a strong finish took him to within a nose of victory against an all-the-way winner in the Cambridgeshire at Newmarket (1m1f, good to firm) on final outing; raised 4lb after that big-race performance but should still be of major interest if tuned up for this return.
runners.comment: Strong finish took him to within a nose of victory in the 1m1f Cambridgeshire at Newmarket

runners.name: Lir Speciale
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a heavy-ground 7f double at Doncaster; better form this season when third of 22 to Mezcala in the Spring Mile at same track (good to soft) and close third at Chester (7.6f, good) ten days ago, but he's edging back up the weights.
runners.comment: 7yo who's in his best form for ages but is edging back up the weights

runners.name: Hard Endeavor
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of ability on all four starts (AW/good) as 3yo, winning a 1m novice, but he seems to have been hit hard by the handicapper following his fifth of seven last November in a 9.5f Listed race on Deauville AW.
runners.comment: Only four races; seems to have been hit hard for his fifth of seven in French Listed race

runners.name: Checkandchallenge
runners.spotlight: 7yo who's spent the vast majority of his career in Group or Listed races and this time last year he was fifth on this card in the Group 1 Lockinge; only his second handicap when, like so many others, he met traffic problems in the Spring Cup over C&D (28-1) four weeks ago; Rossa Ryan gets back on board and they are not totally dismissed.
runners.comment: 7yo who's spent vast majority of his career in Group or Listed races; 5th in 2025 Lockinge

runners.name: Witch Hunter
runners.spotlight: His only turf wins came in a Royal Ascot handicap and Newbury Group 3, but they were in 2023 and he needs the cards to drop right for his late bids; scored on AW in January but subsequent form, including when 40-1 in the Victoria Cup last Saturday, isn't good enough.
runners.comment: No turf win since 2023; AW win in January but subsequent form isn't good enough

runners.name: Ebt's Guard
runners.spotlight: Tough and consistent sort who won the C&D Spring Cup on 2025 reappearance but couldn't get a clear run in it this time round; third in this race last year and 9lb higher today but he's an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Won C&D Spring Cup in 2025; couldn't get a clear run in it in 2026; 3rd in this last year

runners.name: Green Triangle
runners.spotlight: Held up of late; competitive if judged on his peak Irish form for Joseph O'Brien, which was a 9.5f win last June in first-time blinkers; has played minor roles for new yard since, with some promise on occasions at Meydan (1m1f-1m4f) this year but others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Promise on occasions at Meydan (1m1f-1m4f) this year but others have more pressing claims

runners.name: Houquetot
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in France as a 2yo in 2024; below best figures in AW handicap and Meydan Group 2 during the winter on his only two starts for David O'Meara; needs to prove he retains full ability and, unless the betting speaks favourably, best watched on this debut for another new yard.
runners.comment: Ex-French; needs to prove he retains full ability on this debut for another new yard

races.race_name: World Pool Bet With The tote Handicap

runners.name: Lake Como
runners.spotlight: Respectable fourth in Newmarket handicap (1m) last time, without quite transferring his broadly progressive AW form back to turf; again has frame possibilities off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive on AW; respectable fourth at Newmarket returned to turf; still in form

runners.name: Lost Signal
runners.spotlight: Progressive at 6f-7.4f and his 2yo form has substance; ran well at the weights in conditions race at Newmarket (good) on reappearance; looks well treated off an unchanged mark on handicap debut; big player, provided he stays this new trip.
runners.comment: Progressive and looks well treated off an unchanged mark on handicap debut; big player

runners.name: Zennor Storm
runners.spotlight: Two from two as a 2yo, both starts on Kempton AW; last of nine in Newmarket handicap (1m) on reappearance but may do better with the outing under his belt; by no means written off.
runners.comment: 2-2 as a 2yo, both starts on AW; last of nine in Newmarket handicap on reappearance

runners.name: Sapphire Steps
runners.spotlight: Filly whose Newbury record features her debut success last July (6f) and creditable fifth on handicap/seasonal debut (7f, good; Class 2); in the mix, provided she stays this new trip.
runners.comment: Filly who has respectable Newbury form, including reappearance effort; in the mix
runners.owner: J Palmer-Brown, C Engel And Partner

runners.name: Arbaawy
runners.spotlight: Maiden who is proving consistent and ran creditably at Ascot (1m, good) most recently; should give his running but chance of winning largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden; chance of winning largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Law Court
runners.spotlight: Made the frame at Newbury (6.5f/7f) in both 2yo starts and went down by only a neck at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on reappearance; fairly solid handicap debutant, assuming he stays the new trip.
runners.comment: Made the frame at Newbury in both 2yo starts; close second in AW event on reappearance

runners.name: Euston Hall
runners.spotlight: Prevailed narrowly in Yarmouth maiden (1m, good to firm) on seasonal debut, building on his 2yo AW efforts; this thrice-raced colt looks the type who could improve further and do well in handicaps; respected.
runners.comment: Looks the type who could build on Yarmouth maiden win and do well in handicaps; respected

runners.name: Magnatura
runners.spotlight: Absent since a Goodwood success (7f, good) that took his 2yo record to 2-5; faces a tougher task off revised mark in this field but may improve further; market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Absent since a Goodwood win that took his 2yo record to 2-5; may improve further

runners.name: Media Legend
runners.spotlight: Showed ability over 7f on AW last year, notably when close second (in front of Euston Hall) at Kempton; gelded since last run; may be capable of progress now handicapping in a new scenario; one to consider.
runners.comment: May be capable of progress now handicapping in a new scenario; one to consider

runners.name: Tavana
runners.spotlight: Showed fairly useful form over 7f last term, including success in Newbury conditions race (good to soft) against her own sex; sister to a 1m winner; could go well, returned to this venue.
runners.comment: Bred to stay this new trip; could go well returned to the scene of her conditions race win

runners.name: Chapter
runners.spotlight: Still quite unexposed but needs to prove his heavy-ground handicap form (win/third; both 7f) can be transferred back to forecast faster conditions; gelded since those autumn efforts.
runners.comment: Chance may depend on the weather's actions; has shown his handicap form on heavy ground

runners.name: Loz Vegas
runners.spotlight: Seemed to run well in notable conditions race over C&D on penultimate 2yo start but needs to prove he can back up that form, hence could be on a difficult mark; gelded over the winter.
runners.comment: Needs to prove his apparent best 2yo effort can be backed up; gelded over the winter
